Opening Remarks
Rewiring the DNA of Banking for an Innovation‑First Era
session_description: As financial services enter a phase defined by speed, scale, and constant reinvention, banks can no longer innovate at the edges. This session explores how core banking models, culture, and technology stacks must be fundamentally re-engineered to support continuous innovation, ecosystem partnerships, and customer-centric growth in the lead-up to 2030.

session_title: Opening Remarks
Capital Markets: Shaping the Future of Finance
session_description: As capital markets undergo rapid transformation, driven by digitalization, decentralization, and evolving regulatory frameworks, they are redefining how capital is raised, allocated, and accessed globally.

session_title: Investor Readiness for FinTech Startups: Pitching to Banks & Funds
session_description: Startups often have groundbreaking ideas but struggle to secure funding. This keynote equips fintech entrepreneurs with actionable strategies for engaging investors, from banks to venture funds. Learn what investors look for, how to structure compelling pitches, and how to translate innovation into credible business propositions that capture capital and attention.

session_title: The Future of Private Capital: Navigating Change, Risk, and Opportunity
session_description: Explore how private capital is evolving in a rapidly changing market. This keynote examines global debt dynamics, regulatory pressures, and investor priorities while highlighting how fintech innovations—AI, data analytics, and digital lending platforms—are reshaping private credit. Attendees will gain a forward-looking view of opportunities and risks in the evolving landscape.

session_title: Banking Transformed: Digital Public Infrastructure for Resilient Finance
session_description: Robust digital public infrastructure is key to resilient, efficient, and inclusive financial systems. This session explores how financial institutions are leveraging technologies such as digital identity, real-time payment systems, and blockchain to modernize financial infrastructure. Experts will discuss strategies for enhancing transparency, promoting interoperability, and strengthening economic resilience while enabling innovation across public and private sectors.

session_title: UAE's Islamic Finance Leadership: Vision 2031
session_description: This opening address sets the strategic tone for the forum, positioning the UAE as a global hub for Islamic finance innovation, capital markets leadership, and ethical financial transformation.

session_title: Welcome Address
Welcome Address
Private Credit in Motion: Tech-Enabled Megatrends Shaping the Market
session_description: Discover the fintech-driven megatrends transforming private credit globally. Panelists will explore AI-powered risk assessment, tokenized debt, embedded finance, and cross-border lending innovations. This session highlights actionable insights for investors, platforms, and fintech innovators seeking to capitalize on growth opportunities in emerging and established markets.

session_title: The Macro Playbook: Islamic Finance as a Global Stabilizer
session_description: Amid global economic fragmentation, this high-level dialogue explores how the asset-backed nature of Islamic finance acts as a stabilizer for the international financial system.

session_title: Who Owns the Payment? Banks, Fintechs and the Race for the Transaction Layer
session_description: As banks, fintechs, and platforms all compete to own the moment a transaction actually happens, the real prize in payments is shifting from volume to ownership of the transaction layer itself. This panel explores who is actually winning that race, and what it means for pricing power, data, and the customer relationship going forward.

session_title: The Global Paradigm Shift: Beyond Traditional Economic Models
session_description: The global economy is at a turning point, moving beyond traditional growth and development models. In this fireside chat, a leading economist or industry strategist will discuss emerging trends, including technology-driven innovation and sustainability imperatives.

session_title: Tokenized Capital Markets: What VCs Need to Understand Before Private Markets Move On-Chain
session_description: Private markets are moving on-chain faster than most investors have priced in — from tokenized fund structures to on-chain cap tables and secondary liquidity. This session breaks down what's actually happening beneath the hype: which parts of the private markets stack are genuinely being rebuilt, what VCs need to understand about custody, governance, and liquidity before allocating into tokenized structures, and where the real risk sits for funds moving too early — or too late.

session_title: Emerging Technologies Driving FinTech Startups
session_description: From AI and machine learning to blockchain and embedded finance, emerging technologies are reshaping the fintech landscape. This session explores how startups can leverage these innovations to create scalable, market-ready solutions. Attendees will gain insights into the tools and trends powering the next generation of fintech disruption.

session_title: AI and the Next Generation of SMEs
session_description: AI is rapidly becoming the growth engine for small and medium enterprises, reshaping how they access finance, manage operations, and compete globally. This session examines how intelligent tools, data-driven decisioning, and embedded financial services are enabling the next generation of SMEs to scale faster, operate smarter, and participate more fully in the digital economy by 2026 and beyond.

session_title: The Current State of Global Finance: Navigating Economic Shifts in a Multi-Polar World
session_description: Global finance is undergoing a profound transformation as economic power decentralizes and new regional alliances emerge. This session examines how shifting monetary policies, geopolitical dynamics, and evolving capital flows are redefining financial stability and opportunity. Industry leaders will discuss how institutions and fintech innovators are adapting to this multi-polar landscape and what it means for the future of global markets.

session_title: Re-Architecting Market Access in a Digital-First Financial System
session_description: Market access has traditionally been gated by intermediaries, geography, and legacy infrastructure — but a digital-first financial system is quietly dismantling those barriers. This session explores how exchanges, brokers, and market infrastructure providers are being re-architected for a world of real-time settlement, API-driven connectivity, and increasingly global participation. Panellists will examine who benefits as access opens up, who gets disintermediated, and what genuinely new infrastructure — rather than just a digital wrapper on old systems — actually requires.

session_title: From Purpose to Progress: Advancing Towards  a Sustainable Global Future
session_description: A high-level keynote on turning sustainability commitments into real-world action through leadership, innovation, and cross-sector collaboration to shape a resilient global future

session_title: Why Partnerships Win: How Banks, Fintechs & Big Tech Build Faster Together
session_description: No single institution can innovate at the speed today’s financial ecosystem demands. This session explores how strategic partnerships between banks, fintechs, and big tech players are accelerating product development, expanding reach, and unlocking new value—showcasing collaboration as the core operating model for financial innovation in 2026.

session_title: How Global Financial Institutions Are Diversifying for a Fractured World
session_description: As trade, technology, and capital increasingly become instruments of geopolitical leverage, global banks are being forced to rethink where they book business, hold capital, and manage exposure. This fireside chat explores how a CEO leading operations across multiple Asian markets is building genuine diversification — not as a reactive hedge, but as a deliberate resilience strategy for an increasingly fractured world.

session_title: Islamic Finance Meets Global Ethics: Where Sharia, ESG, and Technology Converge
session_description: As global finance increasingly prioritises sustainability, transparency, and ethical outcomes, this panel explores the convergence between Shariah principles, ESG frameworks, and digital innovation.

session_title: From Chatbots to Financial Super-Agents in Core Verticals
session_description: Digital ecosystems offer immense growth opportunities for fintech startups. This panel examines how platforms—from streaming to e-commerce—are integrating financial services, creating network effects, and enabling startups to scale faster. Experts share strategies for embedding fintech into existing ecosystems and driving adoption across diverse consumer bases.

session_title: How Can Banks Unlock New Sources of Growth? A Candid Conversation with CEOs.
session_description: Banks face unprecedented opportunities to grow beyond traditional models. In this candid conversation, industry leaders will share insights on unlocking new revenue streams, leveraging technology, and partnering with fintechs to drive innovation.

session_title: From Rails to Ecosystems: Reimagining Payments in a Platform-Driven Economy
session_description: As payments move beyond traditional rails, they are increasingly embedded within digital platforms, marketplaces, and super-apps. This panel explores how payment providers, banks, and fintechs are evolving from standalone infrastructure players into ecosystem orchestrators. The discussion will examine the rise of embedded finance, real-time payments, cross-border interoperability, and the strategic partnerships shaping the next generation of payment models in a platform-driven global economy.

session_title: From Siloed Products to a Unified Platform: Building Segment-Aware Journeys on a Single Fintech Stack
session_description: This session explores how modern fintechs are breaking down silos to deliver unified, segment-aware journeys on a single digital stack. The discussion focuses on platform architecture, data orchestration, and AI-driven personalization that allow financial institutions to serve diverse customer segments seamlessly, increase engagement, and unlock new monetisation opportunities—turning fragmented offerings into a cohesive, scalable ecosystem.

session_title: Embedded Everything: Where Finance Disappears Into Everyday Life
session_description: Finance is no longer something people seek out - it's built into the apps, marketplaces, and devices they already use. From checkout-page lending to in-app insurance to invisible cross-border payments, embedded finance is quietly becoming the dominant distribution model for financial services. This panel brings together bankers, technology platforms, and investors to explore what happens when finance stops being a destination and becomes a feature

session_title: Wealth-Tech as the Gateway to Inclusive Investing
session_description: WealthTech has spent the last decade making investing cheaper and more accessible — but access alone hasn't closed the participation gap. This session explores how technology is being used to genuinely bring underserved investors into wealth-building, from fractional ownership to AI-driven advisory that scales personalized guidance beyond the traditionally affluent. Panellists will examine what real inclusion looks like beyond lower fees, and where the model still falls short.

session_title: From Exposure to Endurance: Financing Long-Term Resilience Through Policy, Capital and Collaboration
session_description: This session will examine how policy alignment, strategic capital deployment, and cross-sector collaboration can strengthen long-term economic and climate resilience.

session_title: Move Fast and Fix Things: Fintech's Second-Generation Playbook
session_description: Fintech's first generation was built on a simple playbook: move fast, break things, ask forgiveness later. That era is over. This session examines how fintech leaders are rewriting the playbook for a second generation — one that pairs speed with institutional-grade trust, compliance, and operational discipline from day one.This session will explore what's genuinely changed in how fintechs build, scale, and govern themselves, and why the winners this cycle look very different from the disruptors of a decade ago.

session_title: Building the AI-First Islamic Bank
session_description: This keynote explores how artificial intelligence, automation, and advanced data analytics are transforming Islamic banking from the core; beyond mobile apps.

session_title: Payments Revolution Unleashed: Fueling Global Foreign Trade
session_description: This session explores how next-generation payment solutions—leveraging real-time settlement, smart routing, AI-driven FX, and embedded compliance—are transforming global trade. The discussion highlights how fintechs, banks, and platforms are enabling faster, cheaper, and more transparent transactions across markets, unlocking growth for merchants and enterprises while creating new revenue streams and competitive advantages in international commerce.

session_title: Trading at the Speed of Thought: Next-Gen Platform Architecture
session_description: This session explores how next-generation trading platforms are leveraging cloud-native architecture, low-latency infrastructure, real-time analytics, and API-first design to deliver seamless execution for retail and institutional investors alike. The discussion highlights how modern platform engineering, intelligent routing, and resilient systems are transforming trading into a frictionless, scalable, and globally accessible experience—showing investors and operators what it takes to build trading platforms for the markets of 2026 and beyond.

session_title: Reinventing Lending Through Capital Markets Innovation
session_description: Explore how capital markets are reinventing lending for fintechs, shifting from equity-heavy funding to innovative debt instruments that minimize dilution and fuel growth. By leveraging private credit markets, now rivaling public debt at $81 trillion, NAV financing for private equity portfolios, and blended finance with government guarantees, fintechs access scalable capital without ceding control.

session_title: Commercial Speaker Slot
The Liquidity Engine: Building the Rails of Tomorrow’s Value Flows
session_description: This session explores how new liquidity infrastructure — spanning payments, digital assets, market plumbing and regulatory frameworks — is reshaping how value moves across borders and systems. Leaders will examine what it takes to build resilient, interoperable “rails” that can support real-time settlement, scale with global demand, and unlock new forms of economic participation. The discussion looks ahead to who builds these rails, who governs them, and how they will redefine global finance over the next decade.

session_title: Resilient By Design: The UAE's Future Economy
session_description: As global economic power decentralizes, the UAE has spent the last decade building the institutional foundations to compete in whatever comes next. In this fireside chat, senior DIFC leadership reflects on how Dubai's financial ecosystem was deliberately designed for resilience — from regulatory infrastructure to capital markets access — and what it will take to convert that foundation into lasting advantage as the global financial order continues to shift."

session_title: Digital Banking Reinvented: Blueprints for the Next Generation of Financial Institutions
session_description: Traditional banks are at a crossroads: evolve into fully digital institutions or risk losing market share to agile neo-banks. This session explores how leading financial institutions are transforming operations, customer engagement, and product offerings to compete in the digital era. Experts will share strategic blueprints, technology adoption frameworks, and lessons from successful digital-first initiatives set to shape the next generation of finance.

session_title: Tokenization and DeFi: Reshaping Capital Access for Businesses
session_description: Beyond speculation, tokenization and DeFi are quietly creating new ways for businesses to access capital — from tokenized invoices to on-chain credit lines. This session explores which of these models are genuinely reaching real businesses today, and what still stands between pilot and scale.

session_title: De-Risking or De-Globalizing? Building Payment Infrastructure for an Interconnected World
session_description: As global commerce evolves, payment infrastructure is becoming more adaptive, intelligent, and regionally diverse. Financial institutions are rethinking how money moves across borders, building new corridors, strengthening compliance capabilities, and investing in alternative rails. This session will explore how geopolitical shifts are accelerating innovation, reshaping partnerships, and creating new opportunities for more resilient and inclusive global payment networks.

session_title: The "Halal Super-App" Ecosystem: Beyond Banking
session_description: In 2026, the bank is no longer a destination; it is an invisible layer. Explore Banking-as-a-Service (BaaS) models integrating lifestyle, commerce, and finance into a single Shariah-compliant digital journey.

session_title: Trackside VC Bets: From Startup Foals to Unicorn Thoroughbreds
session_description: Spotting payments and lending dark horses that deliver 100x returns by race end. Explores how VCs evaluate, nurture, and accelerate fintech startups from early-stage concepts (“foals”) to high-growth, market-defining “unicorns.” The discussion highlights investment strategies, data-driven due diligence, portfolio management, and ecosystem partnerships that help founders scale sustainably, while providing investors with actionable insights on spotting the next wave of transformative fintech ventures.

session_title: From Insight to Impact: Leveraging AI, Data and Technology for Sustainable Growth
session_description: How organizations are harnessing AI, advanced analytics, and digital technologies to transform sustainability data into actionable insights, driving smarter decisions, operational efficiency, climate resilience, and long-term sustainable growth.

session_title: Web3 Special Situations: Turning Volatile Markets into Investment Potential
session_description: Volatility in digital asset markets isn't just risk — for the right investors, it's opportunity. This session examines how specialist funds are structuring special-situations strategies around distressed tokens, restructurings, and market dislocations that traditional finance can't touch.

session_title: A New Era of Finance: Navigating the Tech-Driven Economy
session_description: Technology is no longer just a tool for finance — it's becoming the architecture finance runs on. This panel brings together leaders navigating that shift firsthand to discuss which technology bets have actually paid off, which hype cycles haven't, and what genuinely separates transformation from surface-level innovation in a tech-driven economy.

session_title: Governing the Intelligent Financial Sector: Regulation for the Age of Agentic AI
session_description: As agentic AI moves from copilots to systems capable of making real financial decisions, regulators face a genuinely new governance challenge. This panel explores what regulation for an agentic AI-powered financial sector actually needs to look like — and whether current frameworks can adapt fast enough to keep pace.

session_title: Building Intelligent Exchanges: Shaping the Smart Market of Tomorrow
Fintech’s Role in Remittances and Regional Commerce: Driving Credit and Inclusion
session_description: In this session, speakers explore how fintech platforms are expanding access to credit and liquidity in cross-border and regional markets. Experts will highlight the role of mobile payments, digital lending, and embedded finance in supporting SMEs, enabling remittances, and driving financial inclusion—showing the tangible impact of private credit flows on local economies.

session_title: Turning Payment Innovation into Competitive Advantage
session_description: As digital commerce accelerates, payments have shifted from a back-end function to a strategic driver of customer experience, revenue, and differentiation. This session explores how organizations can harness innovations such as real-time payments, embedded finance, AI-driven fraud prevention, and seamless cross-border solutions to reduce friction, unlock new revenue streams, and strengthen customer loyalty. Attendees will gain practical insights into aligning payment strategies with business goals, navigating evolving regulatory landscapes, and capitalizing on the trends that matter most right now—from digital wallets to tokenization.

session_title: Networking Coffee Break
Networking Break
Defining the Future of Money: The Next Wave of Innovation
session_description: Money is evolving at an unprecedented pace, driven by technology, regulation, and market innovation. This session explores the next wave of financial innovation, from digital currencies and tokenized assets to programmable payments and fintech-led solutions. Panellists will discuss how these developments are redefining monetary systems, enabling more efficient, inclusive, and secure financial ecosystems, and highlight the opportunities and challenges for institutions, regulators, and consumers in shaping the future of money.

session_title: The Business Model Behind the App: Monetising Neobanking at Scale
session_description: A good app was never the business model — it was the entry point. This session gets specific about how neobanks are actually building revenue at scale: which monetisation strategies are working, which are propping up unprofitable growth, and what a durable neobank P&L really looks like.

session_title: From Open Banking to Open Finance – How Can Banks Seize The Opportunity?
session_description: The brokerage industry is undergoing its biggest shift in a decade. Retail investors expect instant, mobile-first, low-cost access. Institutions want smarter execution, cross-asset access, and better risk tools. And brokers themselves are navigating new asset classes, from digital assets to tokenized realworld assets, while integrating AI across trading, compliance, advisory, and client engagement. This panel brings together leading brokerage and fintech voices to unpack how the next generation of Brokerage is reshaping revenue models, client expectations, and competitive advantage going forward.

session_title: Networking Break
Towards New Financial Corridors: Capital, Technology and Innovation
session_description: As global capital flows evolve, new financial corridors are emerging, powered by technology and innovative business models. This session explores how digital platforms, cross-border investments, and fintech solutions are reshaping the movement of capital and unlocking new market opportunities. Leaders will discuss strategies to leverage technology and innovation to strengthen connectivity, drive growth, and create more inclusive and efficient financial ecosystems.

session_title: Networking Break
The New Economics of Cross-Border Money: The Future of Global Transactions
session_description: Global finance is undergoing a rapid transformation as technology, regulation, and evolving customer expectations reshape how money moves across borders. This session explores the convergence of foreign exchange markets with fintech-driven innovations in payments, remittances, and liquidity management.

session_title: Networking Coffee Break
Shariah Compliance vs Market Reality: Where Do We Draw the Line in Volatile Times?
session_description: A candid and forward-looking debate addressing the tension between Shariah principles and market pressures during periods of volatility.

session_title: Stablecoins as a Core Financial Market-Infrastructure
session_description: Stablecoins have moved well past their origins as a crypto trading tool and are increasingly functioning as core financial market infrastructure — settling trades, moving institutional liquidity, and underpinning cross-border payments. This fireside chat explores what it actually takes for stablecoins to be trusted as genuine infrastructure rather than just a faster payment rail.

session_title: Lunch Break
Localizing FinTech: Designing Products for Every Market
session_description: In an increasingly globalized world, fintech companies face the challenge of scaling innovative products while adapting to the unique needs of diverse regional markets. Explore how financial technology firms can successfully localize their offerings by balancing regulatory compliance, consumer behavior, technology adoption, and financial inclusion. Gain insights into market-specific strategies, through expert advise and uncover how fintechs can design products that resonate with local consumers, drive adoption, and unlock growth opportunities in emerging and established markets alike.

session_title: Why FinTech Innovation Must Convert: From Interest to Revenue
session_description: Innovation alone isn’t enough—success comes when fintech solutions deliver measurable business outcomes. This keynote highlights strategies to convert early interest into revenue, from product-market fit and user adoption to monetization models that drive sustainable growth.

session_title: Tokenization at Scale: The Next Trillion-Dollar Asset Class
session_description: Tokenization has moved past pilot programs into genuine institutional deployment, with projections pointing toward a trillion-dollar asset class within reach. This session examines which asset types are actually scaling on-chain today, what infrastructure and regulatory pieces are still missing, and what it will take for tokenized assets to become a durable, mainstream part of the financial system rather than a parallel niche.

session_title: Networking Break
Women Driving the Cutting Edge of Finance
session_description: This fireside chat explores how women leaders are shaping the next frontier of finance, from digital transformation and emerging technologies to new models of leadership and governance. The conversation will unpack the opportunities and barriers facing women at the forefront of innovation, and what it takes to build inclusive, high-performing financial institutions in a rapidly evolving landscape. Attendees will gain insights into how diverse leadership is not just changing who leads finance, but how the industry itself is being redefined.

session_title: Smarter Risk, Smarter Insurance: Turning Insurtech Innovation into Profitable Protection
session_description: Insurtech is enabling insurers to move beyond protection toward smarter risk selection, pricing, and prevention. This session highlights how digital distribution, advanced analytics, and ecosystem partnerships are helping insurers improve profitability, launch new products faster, and meet evolving customer expectations in a data-driven insurance economy.

session_title: From ESG Disclosure to Strategic Advantage: Turning Standards into Investable Outcomes
session_description: This discussion focuses on how credible ESG reporting and global standards are moving beyond compliance to unlock trust, capital access, and bankable opportunities.

session_title: Lunch Break
Building Seamless Payment Infrastructure Across Borders
session_description: As commerce becomes increasingly global, fragmented payment systems remain a major bottleneck to growth. This session examines how modern payment infrastructure—driven by interoperability, real-time settlement, and regulatory coordination—is enabling faster, more efficient cross-border money movement and redefining how value flows across markets by 2026.

session_title: Lunch Break
AI & ML in Action: How Data Drives FinTech Localization
session_description: As fintech companies expand into diverse markets, understanding local customer behavior and adapting products accordingly is key to success. Artificial intelligence, machine learning, and advanced data analytics are transforming the way financial products are designed, personalized, and delivered in different regions. From real-world examples of predictive credit scoring, personalized wallets, and mobile-first solutions, highlighting how data-driven insights enable fintechs to serve underserved populations, optimize adoption, and innovate responsibly.

session_title: Who Controls the Future of Money: AI, Geopolitics, and the Rewiring of Global Finance
session_description: Money is being reshaped by two forces at once - AI increasingly deciding how capital is deployed, and geopolitics increasingly deciding what rails it moves through. This session asks which force actually holds more power over the future of finance, and what that means for institutions caught between the two.

session_title: Commercial Speaker Slot
Data-First, Digital-Led: Building a Modern Banking Powerhouse
session_description: Behind every 'digital-first' banking claim is a harder question: what does it actually take to run a bank on data rather than just presenting a good app? This fireside chat explores how one of the region's leading banking groups has built a genuinely data-first operating model — from underwriting to customer engagement — and what separates real digital transformation from a modernized interface on legacy infrastructure.

session_title: The "Digital Amanah" - Automating Shariah Compliance
session_description: This keynote introduces the concept of the “Digital Amanah”: embedding Shariah principles directly into operational infrastructure through automation, data, and governance-by-design.

session_title: Borderless Payments Unleashed: AI + Biometrics at Scale
session_description: As payments go instant and global, identity verification has to keep pace without adding friction. This session explores how AI and biometric authentication are being combined to enable secure, borderless payments at scale — and what it takes to deploy this reliably across jurisdictions.

session_title: Funding, Partnerships & What Excites Investors in FinTech
session_description: Gain exclusive insights from leading investors on the trends, sectors, and metrics that attract capital in fintech. Panelists will discuss partnership opportunities, funding strategies, and the key factors they consider when evaluating startups. Attendees will leave with practical guidance on positioning their ventures for investment and growth.

session_title: From Proof-of-Concept to Production: Why Most FinTech Pilots Fail
session_description: Bringing a fintech solution from pilot to full-scale production is fraught with challenges. This fireside chat uncovers common pitfalls, lessons learned, and best practices for scaling fintech pilots successfully. Experts share real-world examples to help startups navigate operational hurdles and achieve market-ready execution.

session_title: Beyond the Paper Policy: The Rise of "Embedded & Invisible" Takaful
session_description: In 2026, Takaful is no longer just "sold"; it is "embedded." This session explores integration into digital ecosystems (travel apps, e-commerce, neobanks) for protection at the moment of need.

session_title: Global VC Landscape: Where the Exits (and the Money) Are Actually Going
session_description: A grounded read on where global liquidity is really flowing in 2026 — IPOs, M&A, or the exploding secondaries market — and what that means for where LPs and GPs should be positioning next.

session_title: Turning Smartphones into Banks: Mobile Solutions for Inclusive Finance
session_description: This session explores how mobile-first fintech solutions are transforming the way underserved populations engage with finance. Focusing on scalable mobile platforms, digital wallets, embedded lending, and AI-driven personalization, the discussion highlights how turning smartphones into banking hubs can drive inclusion, expand markets, and unlock new revenue opportunities for fintechs, banks, and ecosystem partners.

session_title: Financial Data in the AI Era: Who Really Owns It?
session_description: As AI models are increasingly trained and fine-tuned on financial data, a genuinely unresolved question is emerging: who actually owns that data, and who has the right to profit from it? This panel examines the data ownership, consent, and monetization questions that AI is forcing the financial industry to confront.

session_title: Fintech Innovation & Next Big Bet: New Tech That Will Shape The Future
session_description: Every cycle produces a handful of technologies that actually change the industry, and a lot of noise that doesn't. This session brings together investors and innovators to cut through the hype and identify where the next genuine step-change in fintech is likely to come from.

session_title: Open banking: a catalyst for financial inclusion and market competition
session_description: Open banking is reshaping the financial services landscape by unlocking data, fostering innovation, and enabling greater collaboration across the ecosystem. This session explores how open banking frameworks are driving financial inclusion by expanding access to underserved populations while simultaneously intensifying competition and accelerating the development of customer-centric products. Attendees will gain insights into how organizations can leverage open APIs, partnerships, and data-driven models to create new value, navigate regulatory complexities, and compete effectively in a rapidly evolving financial environment.

session_title: How Can Businesses Deploy Innovation at Scale and Responsibly? Scaling AI: Now is where the real work begins.
session_description: Scaling a fintech from startup to global leader requires vision, agility, and bold decision-making. Top fintech CEO's shares first-hand insights into driving growth, fostering innovation, and navigating hyper-competitive and rapidly evolving markets. Gain a rare look into strategic priorities, leadership challenges, and the lessons learned from building a successful fintech in today’s digital-first world.

session_title: Lunch Break
Accelerating the Energy Transition: Scaling Clean Power, Hydrogen and Sustainable Infrastructure
session_description: This session explores how governments, investors and industry leaders are accelerating the deployment of clean energy, hydrogen and sustainable infrastructure to strengthen energy security, drive decarbonization and enable long-term economic growth.

session_title: Networking Lunch
Unlocking the Future of Wealth Management in the Era of Intelligent, Inclusive Innovation
session_description: The wealth management industry is evolving in an era of intelligent and inclusive innovation. This session explores how technology—AI, analytics, and digital platforms—is reshaping investment strategies, client engagement, and operational efficiency.

session_title: Lunch Break
Beyond the Hype: Building Stablecoin Rails Without Increasing Institutional Risk
session_description: Stablecoins promise faster, cheaper settlement, but institutions adopting them are also inheriting new categories of risk — custody, reserve transparency, and counterparty exposure chief among them. This fireside chat cuts through the hype to examine what genuinely risk-managed stablecoin infrastructure looks like for institutional adopters.

session_title: Hedge That Position: Smarter Investing in the Digital Brokerage Era
session_description: Digital brokerages have made trading faster and cheaper, but risk management hasn't always kept pace with access. This session explores how retail and institutional investors alike are using smarter, tech-enabled hedging and risk tools in an era of instant execution.

session_title: Unlocking Growth in a Dynamic Market: Innovations in Investment
session_description: Amid evolving markets and shifting investor expectations, innovation is key to unlocking new growth opportunities. This session explores how technology, data-driven strategies, and alternative investment models are reshaping capital allocation, portfolio management, and risk assessment. Industry leaders will share insights on emerging trends, fintech-driven investment solutions, and strategies to navigate dynamic market conditions while maximizing returns.

session_title: Networking Lunch
Lunch Break
AI-Driven Payments: The Future of Secure, Scalable, and Seamless Transactions
session_description: AI is reshaping the payments landscape, enabling secure, scalable, and seamless transactions. In this fireside chat, a leading expert will explore how AI-driven solutions are enhancing fraud detection, optimizing transaction flows, and creating smarter, more efficient payment systems.

session_title: Lunch Break
When BNPL Stops Being a Feature and Becomes a Revenue Driver
session_description: Buy Now, Pay Later has evolved from a checkout add-on into a powerful monetisation engine embedded across commerce and financial platforms. This session explores how next-generation BNPL models—driven by data, dynamic risk pricing, and embedded credit infrastructure—are unlocking new revenue streams for merchants, platforms, and financial institutions. Positioned in the Innovation Zone, the discussion focuses on scalable BNPL architectures, real-world deployments, and how BNPL is being redesigned to deliver sustainable growth and profitability by 2026.

session_title: A Rising New Paradigm: Private Markets vs. Public Markets
session_description: Private and public markets are evolving at different speeds, offering distinct opportunities and challenges for investors. This session examines the rising prominence of private markets relative to public markets, exploring factors driving investor interest, risk-return dynamics, and capital allocation strategies.

session_title: The Global Sukuk Market: What Will Unlock the Next Trillion?
session_description: This panel examines the future growth of the global Sukuk market, focusing on new issuer types, investor diversification, product innovation, and regulatory harmonisation.

session_title: Wealth Without Borders: Serving the Next Generation of Global Investors
session_description: As the next generation of global investors increasingly expects seamless, borderless access to wealth management, traditional models built around single-market relationships are being tested. This session explores how leading institutions are redesigning wealth management to genuinely serve clients who invest, transact, and live across multiple markets at once.

session_title: Policy Priorities: Building the Next Generation Digital Economy
session_description: As digital finance matures from experimentation to core infrastructure, policymakers face a widening set of priorities — from data governance to cross-border interoperability to competition policy. This fireside chat brings a policy lens to the digital economy conversation, examining what regulators should actually prioritize to enable sustainable, inclusive growth.

session_title: Transforming Credit: Neo-Banks Driving Digital Lending
session_description: Digital-first neo-banks are redefining the credit landscape by leveraging data, automation, and seamless user experiences to deliver faster, more inclusive lending solutions. This session explores how these challengers are disrupting traditional credit models through alternative data, AI-driven underwriting, and fully digital customer journeys, expanding access while improving speed and efficiency. Attendees will gain insights into how organizations can adapt to this shift, harness emerging technologies, and compete in a lending environment where agility, personalization, and real-time decisioning are becoming the new standard.

session_title: Emerging Markets, Established Ambitions: Where Global Capital Is Really Flowing
session_description: Institutional capital is increasingly flowing toward emerging markets in search of growth and diversification. This session examines where that capital is actually going, what's driving allocation decisions, and how emerging market institutions are competing for global attention.

session_title: Building Resilient Cities: Sustainable Mobility, Smart Infrastructure and Urban Transformation
session_description: This session examines how innovative mobility solutions, smart infrastructure and integrated urban planning are shaping resilient, low-carbon and future-ready cities.

session_title: Global Financial Stability in an Era of Technological Transformation
session_description: As AI, tokenization, and instant settlement reshape how the financial system actually functions, regulators and institutions are being forced to ask whether traditional stability frameworks still hold. This closing session examines where genuine systemic risk is building in an increasingly technology-driven financial system, and what safeguarding stability actually requires going forward.

session_title: From Protection to Prediction: Insurtech for a Risk-Intelligent World
session_description: Insurance is shifting from reactive protection to proactive risk intelligence. This session explores how data, AI, and connected technologies are enabling insurers to anticipate risk, personalize coverage, and prevent losses—positioning insurtech as a critical pillar of a smarter, more resilient financial system by 2026.

session_title: Commercial Speaker Slot
The New Silk Road: Interoperability as the Engine for Next  Economic Decade
session_description: As trade and capital increasingly route through new corridors bypassing traditional Western infrastructure, interoperability — not size or geography — is becoming the defining advantage for the next decade of global finance. This session explores what it actually takes to build genuinely interoperable financial infrastructure that can plug into multiple regional systems at once, rather than forcing the world to standardize on any single one.

session_title: Crypto as a norm: Making Blockchain Disappear Into Everyday Financial Life
session_description: For crypto to reach true mass adoption, it needs to stop feeling like crypto at all. This fireside chat explores the philosophy of building blockchain-based financial products so seamless that using them feels like ordinary banking — where the technology disappears into the background and users simply experience faster, more open finance.

session_title: Crypto Sukuk & Tokenised Markets: Innovation or Inflection Point?
session_description: An edge-of-the-seat conversation blending Islamic finance with future capital markets, grounded in regulation and investor protection, exploring tokenized Shariah assets.

session_title: Scaling Micro-Credit Globally: Platforms, Risk Models, and Product Innovation
session_description: Financial inclusion in rural and underserved regions is being rapidly advanced through mobile wallets, micro-lending platforms, and localized banking solutions. This session examines strategies fintechs are using to reach these populations, from designing mobile-first products to deploying innovative micro-loans that address local needs. Experts will share real-world examples of how technology is bridging gaps in access, enabling secure, convenient financial services, and driving economic empowerment in communities that have historically been excluded from traditional banking. Attendees will gain actionable insights into building scalable, inclusive fintech solutions tailored for local markets.

session_title: Fraud Is Getting Smarter — So Must Payments
session_description: Fraud tactics are evolving as fast as the payment systems built to stop them, increasingly powered by the same AI tools defenders rely on. This session examines how payment providers are staying ahead of an adversary that learns in real time.

session_title: Networking Coffee Break
Reaching the Next Billion: Innovations in Digital Lending
session_description: As financial institutions look beyond saturated markets, the next wave of growth lies in reaching underserved and emerging segments at scale. This session explores how innovations in digital lending—powered by alternative data, mobile-first platforms, and AI-driven credit models—are expanding access to finance for individuals and small businesses traditionally excluded from the system. Attendees will gain insights into how to design inclusive, scalable lending solutions, overcome infrastructure and regulatory challenges, and unlock new opportunities in high-growth markets while balancing risk, trust, and profitability.

session_title: Innovation Without Borders: Scaling Finance for the Global South
session_description: Financial innovation built for mature markets doesn't always translate to the Global South's infrastructure, regulatory, and customer realities. This session explores what it actually takes to scale fintech and financial services in these markets on their own terms,

session_title: Banking for Everyone: Unlocking Financial Access at Scale
session_description: As financial inclusion moves from ambition to execution, scale has become the defining challenge. This session explores how digital infrastructure, alternative data, and ecosystem partnerships are enabling financial institutions to expand access responsibly—bringing affordable, trusted financial services to broader populations in the global economy of 2026.

session_title: Consolidation Is Coming: What Fintech's Next Phase Looks Like
session_description: As easy capital dries up, this session looks past the "sustainable growth" buzzwords to the real consolidation wave reshaping the fintech landscape. Panellists will examine which business models are actually built to survive a tighter funding environment, where the next wave of M&A and acquihires is likely to hit, and what founders and investors alike need to rethink about scale, profitability, and exit timing. The conversation will offer a candid, non-consensus read on who consolidates, who gets acquired, and who simply disappears.

session_title: Closing Remarks
The Global Sustainability Transition: Aligning Business Models with a Net-Zero Future
session_description: This session will focus on how global businesses must redesign models, capital allocation, and governance to compete, comply, and create value in a net-zero economy.

session_title: The One-Click Economy: What People Really Want from Finance
session_description: Beneath every feature war in fintech is a simpler question: what do people actually want from their financial services? This session cuts through the roadmap noise to explore what genuinely drives adoption and loyalty — speed, simplicity, trust — and why the 'one-click' promise so often falls short in practice.

session_title: The New Capital Wave: Private Credit in the Global South
session_description: Private credit is reshaping financing in emerging markets, offering flexible alternatives where traditional bank lending is limited. This session explores the growth of private debt in the Global South, highlighting opportunities for institutional investors, corporates, and development-focused funds. Here we will discuss risk frameworks, market dynamics, regulatory considerations, and case studies of successful capital deployment.

session_title: The Intelligent Bank: Redesigning Finance for an AI-First World
session_description: As AI moves from isolated tools to the core architecture banks run on, a genuinely 'AI-first' institution looks fundamentally different from one that simply bolted AI onto legacy systems. This panel explores what it actually takes to redesign a bank's products, decisioning, and operations around AI from the ground up, and what separates real transformation from AI theatre.

session_title: Alpha in a Fragmented World: How Active and Alternative Managers Are Re-Engineering Returns
session_description: As markets fragment along geopolitical, regulatory, and liquidity lines, traditional beta exposure is proving insufficient. This session examines how active and alternative managers are adapting portfolio construction, exploiting dislocations, and deploying differentiated strategies to generate durable alpha in a structurally more complex global environment.

session_title: Commercial Speaker Slot
The New Issuers: Why Private Firms are Pivoting to Sukuk
session_description: This session explores why private corporates and family-owned businesses are increasingly turning to Sukuk for funding. The discussion will focus on diversification, cost of capital, investor access, and strategic positioning.

session_title: Networking Break
Networking Break
Closing Remarks
Solving Today’s Financial Infrastructure Bottlenecks
session_description: Behind every fintech announcement is infrastructure that still doesn't quite talk to itself — legacy cores, fragmented APIs, and settlement systems built for a slower era. This session gets practical about where the real bottlenecks sit today, and what it actually takes to clear them.

session_title: Closing Remarks
AI vs. Human Judgment: Balancing Fraud Detection and Customer Trust
session_description: AI vs. Human Judgment: Balancing Fraud Detection and Customer Trust explores the frontline battle where machine intelligence processes billions of signals in milliseconds, yet human insight catches the subtle cues that preserve relationships and prevent costly errors.This session debates optimal hybrid models—when AI should auto-block, when humans intervene, and how to minimize false positives that drive churn. Leaders reveal playbooks for scaling fraud teams, regulatory compliance, and trust-building tech that turns defense into a competitive edge.

session_title: Networking Break
Sukuk Supercharged: Green, Transition, and Tech Hybrids
session_description: A deep-dive on the evolution of Sukuk into green, sustainability-linked, and technology-enabled instruments. The session highlights innovation, investor demand, and impact alignment.

session_title: Financing the Future: Sustainable Investments, Transition Finance, and Capital Allocation Strategies
session_description: This session explores how sustainable investments and transition finance are reshaping capital allocation across industries. It will examine the role of financial institutions, policymakers, and corporates in mobilising capital toward low-carbon growth, resilient economies, and long-term value creation.

session_title: CX-Driven Innovation: Lessons from SuperApps and Embedded Finance
session_description: This session explores how superapps and embedded finance platforms are leveraging seamless CX to drive engagement, adoption, and monetisation. By examining real-world examples, the discussion highlights strategies for designing integrated financial journeys, embedding services into daily life, and turning customer-centric experiences into scalable growth and competitive advantage for fintechs, banks, and ecosystem partners.

session_title: Cross-Border Payments & Embedded Finance in Emerging Markets
session_description: As fintech adoption accelerates across emerging markets, scaling solutions across borders presents both opportunities and challenges. This session explores how companies are navigating cross-border payments, embedded finance, and regional regulatory frameworks in Africa, Latin America, and Southeast Asia. Hear from industry leaders on winning strategies for seamless expansion, navigating diverse regulations, and capturing new markets.

session_title: Taking Islamic Finance to the World: What Comes Next
session_description: A closing strategic conversation on the next phase of global Islamic finance growth — new markets, new audiences, and new delivery models.

session_title: Embedded Insurance: The New Growth Engine for Fintech and Insurers
session_description: Insurance is increasingly showing up exactly where customers need it — at checkout, at signup, inside the products they're already buying. This session explores how embedded insurance is becoming a genuine growth engine for both insurers and the platforms distributing it.

session_title: Closing Remarks
How Private Credit Is Expanding Lending Horizons
session_description: As traditional lending channels tighten, private credit is emerging as a vital source of capital for businesses and consumers alike. This session explores how private credit markets are broadening access to financing, offering flexible structures, and reshaping the global lending landscape—positioning alternative capital as a key growth driver by 2026.

session_title: Executive Takeaways & Strategic Insights: The Future of Financial Services

speakers.name: Gauri Singh
speakers.about: Gauri Singh brings 30+ years of experience shaping renewable energy policy and sustainable development across government and global institutions. She led India’s National Solar Mission and advances international clean energy frameworks, partnerships, and country-level transition strategies.

speakers.name: Nadia Humphreys
speakers.about: Nadia is a global sustainable finance and ESG data expert shaping how investors use climate insights for decision-making. She advises regulators across Europe and Asia, with prior experience at Bloomberg LP, JPMorgan Chase, and Goldman Sachs
speakers.designation: Global Head Sustainable Finance Data Solutions

speakers.name: Manosij Ganguli
speakers.about: Manosij Ganguli is a global ESG and decarbonization leader with experience across energy, mobility, and logistics. A Cambridge graduate, he has driven large-scale sustainability transformations at BP, Shell, and MPP, aligning ESG strategy with operational performance and long-term value creation.

speakers.name: Fazil Abdul Rahiman
speakers.organisation: Abu Dhabi National Energy Company PJSC (TAQA)

speakers.name: Manal Hassan
speakers.about: Manal Hassan is a Forbes Middle East Sustainability Leader and one of MENA’s top 100 sustainability leaders. With 30+ years across finance, ESG, and development, she drives board-level sustainability strategy, net-zero transition, and value-led ESG integration across industry.

speakers.name: Dr Yianni Spanos
speakers.about: Dr Spanos is a sustainability and energy transition leader with 25+ years’ experience delivering decarbonisation strategies across major urban developments. He advises global projects on ESG integration, climate action, and resilient design, bridging technical delivery with long-term value creation.

speakers.name: H.E. Essa Kazim
speakers.about: His Excellency Essa Kazim is the Governor of Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC), Chairman of Borse Dubai, Deputy Chairman of the Supreme Legislation Committee in Dubai, and a member of the Dubai Supreme Fiscal Committee. He plays a central role in shaping Dubai’s financial ecosystem, capital markets strategy, and legislative frameworks. Known for his leadership in financial market development and regulatory governance, H.E. Kazim brings a strategic perspective on global financial centres and market infrastructure.

speakers.name: Noel Quinn
speakers.about: Noel Quinn is the Chairman of the Board of Directors of Julius Baer Group, with over 37 years of experience in global banking and financial services. He previously served as Group Chief Executive of HSBC, where he led the organization for five years and guided its global strategy and transformation. Prior to this, he held senior leadership roles including CEO Global Commercial Banking, Regional Head of Commercial Banking Asia Pacific, and Head of Commercial Banking UK. Known for his strategic leadership in global banking, Quinn brings deep insights into international finance and institutional growth.
speakers.designation: Chairman of the Board of Directors

speakers.name: Jenny Johnson
speakers.about: Jenny Johnson is the Chief Executive Officer of Franklin Templeton, one of the world’s largest global investment managers with $1.7 trillion in assets under management. With a career spanning more than 35 years, she has been a central force in transforming the firm into a diversified platform across public and private asset classes. Before becoming CEO in February 2020, she held senior leadership roles across investment management, distribution, technology, operations, and wealth management. Under her leadership, Franklin Templeton has expanded its global capabilities through strategic acquisitions and investments, strengthening its position as a leading multi-asset investment manager.

speakers.name: Tan Su Shan
speakers.about: Ms Tan Su Shan is the Chief Executive Officer and Director of DBS Group, Southeast Asia’s largest bank. With over 35 years of experience across consumer banking, wealth management, and institutional banking, she previously served as Deputy CEO and Group Head of Institutional Banking, and earlier led Consumer Banking and Wealth Management for nearly a decade. She has also held leadership roles at Morgan Stanley, Citibank, and ING Barings. Recognized globally, she ranked 6th on Fortune’s Most Powerful Women in Business list in 2025 and was featured on Forbes Asia’s Power Businesswomen list in 2024.

speakers.name: Kentaro Okuda
speakers.about: Kentaro Okuda is the President and Group Chief Executive Officer of Nomura Holdings and President of Nomura Securities, and serves as a member of the Board of Directors of Nomura Holdings. With a long-standing career at Nomura since 1987, he has held senior leadership roles across global investment banking, including Global Head of Investment Banking, Global Head of M&A, and Joint Head of Wholesale. Previously, he served as Group Co-COO and President & CEO of Nomura Holding America, leading the firm’s Americas business. Known for his leadership in global capital markets and investment banking, Okuda brings deep insights into international financial markets and institutional strategy.

speakers.name: Ryan Breslow
speakers.about: Ryan Breslow is the Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Bolt, a leading checkout technology company reimagining how people buy, sell, and move money through one-click commerce and crypto-enabled payments. He has founded and led multiple ventures across fintech, digital commerce, and decentralized finance, including Love.com, Prism, Eco, and The Family Accelerator. Known for building next-generation payment and financial platforms, Breslow brings a founder-led perspective on the future of commerce, fintech, and digital money.

speakers.name: Shri Ashishkumar Chauhan
speakers.about: Shri Ashishkumar Chauhan is the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of the National Stock Exchange of India (NSE), with decades of experience in capital markets, technology, and financial infrastructure. A founding member of NSE, he has been instrumental in shaping India’s modern markets, including screen-based trading, the NIFTY index, derivatives markets, and digital trading platforms. Previously, he served as MD and CEO of BSE Limited and held leadership roles across Reliance Group and other institutions. Recognized globally for his impact on financial markets innovation, he is regarded as a pioneer of modern financial derivatives in India.

speakers.name: H.E. Arif Amiri
speakers.about: H. E. Arif Amiri is the Chief Executive Officer of DIFC Authority where he is responsible for setting the strategy and growth direction of the Authority. Under his leadership, DIFC has emerged as a powerful catalyst for the financial services sector, as well as the leading global financial centre in the Middle East, Africa and South Asia (MEASA) region.

speakers.name: Chris Hogbin
speakers.about: Chris Hogbin is the Chief Executive Officer of Lazard Asset Management, appointed in 2025. He joined from AllianceBernstein, where he served as Global Head of Investments, overseeing public and private market strategies, and previously as Head of Equities, leading the growth of the firm’s largest asset class. Earlier in his career, he was a strategy consultant at the Boston Consulting Group across London, San Francisco, and Shanghai. Mr. Hogbin holds an MA in Economics from the University of Cambridge and an MBA with distinction from Harvard Business School, and serves as a Trustee of The Public Theater and President of The Caius Foundation.

speakers.name: Nicolas Moreau
speakers.about: Nicolas Moreau is the Chief Executive Officer of HSBC Asset Management, where he leads the global strategy and growth of HSBC’s investment management business across international markets. With over 30 years of experience in asset management and insurance, he previously served on the Management Board of Deutsche Bank, leading Deutsche Asset Management through its listing and rebranding as DWS. Prior to that, he held multiple senior leadership roles across the AXA Group, including CEO of AXA Investment Managers and Chairman & CEO of AXA France. Recognized for his contributions to the financial industry, he was awarded La Légion d’Honneur in 2015.

speakers.name: Martin Kocher
speakers.about: Martin Kocher is the Governor of the Oesterreichische Nationalbank and a member of the Governing Council of the European Central Bank (ECB). He previously served as Austria’s Federal Minister of Labour and Economy, following his tenure as Federal Minister of Labour. Prior to his government roles, he was Scientific Director of the Institute for Advanced Studies, President of the Fiscal Advisory Council, and Chairman of the Statistics Council. A Full Professor of Economics at the University of Vienna, he has held academic positions across Europe and Australia. Known for his expertise in economic policy and macroeconomic research, Kocher brings a policy and central banking perspective on economic and monetary frameworks.

speakers.name: Sundararaman Ramamurthy
speakers.about: Shri Sundararaman Ramamurthy is the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of BSE, where he leads strategy, product innovation, regulatory engagement, and business development. With over four decades of experience in banking and capital markets, he previously served as MD & COO of Bank of America’s India operations and spent nearly 20 years at NSE, playing a key role in the development of derivatives, indices, data, and clearing operations. Known for his leadership in market infrastructure and digital innovation, Ramamurthy brings deep insights into exchange evolution and capital market transformation.

speakers.name: Fatih Karahan
speakers.about: Fatih Karahan has served as Governor of the Central Bank of the Republic of Türkiye (CBRT) since February 3, 2024, Since taking office, Karahan has been widely seen as a continuity figure committed to disinflation and monetary tightening, building on the more orthodox policy direction set by his predecessor amid Turkey's high-inflation environment.
speakers.organisation: Central Bank of the Rupublic of Türkiye

speakers.name: Antoine Chemali
speakers.about: Antoine Chemali is the Chief Executive Officer of BNP Paribas Wealth Management Middle East and Africa, based in Dubai. With extensive experience advising private and institutional clients across the US, Europe, and the GCC, he has led strategic initiatives in asset management, corporate finance, M&A, and investment strategy. Prior to BNP Paribas, he held senior leadership roles, including CEO and board member positions within a group of companies linked to the UAE Royal Family, overseeing diversified investments across banking, real estate, oil and gas, and industry. Antoine is also the co-founder of Digital World Capital, a London-based multi-asset hedge fund focused on technology sectors. He holds degrees in Electrical Engineering from McGill University and Stanford University, and an MBA from INSEAD.
speakers.organisation: BNP Paribas Wealth Management Middle East

speakers.name: Fernando Zandona
speakers.about: Fernando Zandona is a technology and engineering leader at Mambu, with over 20 years of experience at global technology organizations including Amazon and Microsoft. He leverages deep technical expertise from hyper-growth environments to lead Mambu’s global engineering teams, delivering scalable, customer-centric fintech solutions. Known for his leadership in platform engineering, digital transformation, and innovative product development, Fernando brings a technology-driven perspective on building next-generation core banking and fintech infrastructure.

speakers.name: Sergio Furio
speakers.about: Sergio Furio is the Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Creditas, Latin America’s leading asset-backed fintech with $900 million in funding. He also serves as Chairman of Endeavor Brazil and Partner at Actyus Venture Fund, supporting entrepreneurship and investment across the region. With deep expertise in fintech, digital lending, and financial innovation, Furio has built Creditas into a platform that empowers both consumers and businesses. Known for his entrepreneurial vision and strategic leadership, he brings insights on scaling fintech, transforming credit markets, and shaping financial ecosystems in emerging markets.

speakers.name: Raman Bhatia
speakers.about: Raman Bhatia is the Group Chief Executive Officer and Executive Director of Starling Bank Limited and Starling Group Holdings Limited, responsible for leading the business and setting its strategic direction alongside the board. He joined Starling in 2024 from OVO, one of the UK’s largest energy suppliers, where he drove the company’s expansion into green and sustainable technology. Previously, as Head of Digital Bank at HSBC Retail Banking and Wealth Management for the UK and Europe, he led digital innovation, platform development, and risk management. His earlier career spans senior roles at Expedia, HouseTrip, and Bain & Company.

speakers.name: Alex Manson
speakers.about: Alex Manson is the Chief Executive Officer of SC Ventures, the innovation and venture-building arm of Standard Chartered Bank, which he founded in 2018. With over 30 years in global banking, he has shaped corporate and transaction banking across APAC, Europe, and the US. Before SC Ventures, he held senior leadership roles at Standard Chartered, Deutsche Bank, and Credit Suisse, driving growth in lending, derivatives, and structured products. Known for blending strategic vision with innovation, Manson brings a unique perspective on transforming banking through ventures and fintech solutions.

speakers.name: Magnus Grimeland
speakers.about: Magnus Grimeland is the Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Antler, a global venture capital firm that has invested in over 1,800 companies, including unicorns Lovable and Airalo. Recognized as one of the world’s most active venture capital and AI investors, Antler ranks alongside Y Combinator and a16z for seed-stage investments. Before Antler, Grimeland co-founded Zalora, scaling it into Asia’s leading fashion e-commerce platform, and expanded Global Fashion Group across 26 markets. A Harvard graduate, former McKinsey partner, and ex-member of the Norwegian Naval Special Forces, he brings entrepreneurial, operational, and strategic expertise.

speakers.name: Nalin Negi
speakers.about: Nalin Negi is the Chief Executive Officer of BharatPe, where he drives strategic growth, operational excellence, and expansion in India’s fintech ecosystem. With over 20 years of experience in banking, financial services, and payments, he previously served as CFO of SBI Card, leading its IPO in 2020 as the first pure-play credit card company listed in India. Negi has also held leadership roles at American Express, EXL Service, Nestle India, and ITC. Known for his expertise in financial management, strategy, and innovation, he brings insights on scaling digital payments, lending, and financial inclusion.

speakers.name: Roham Gharegozlou
speakers.about: Roham Gharegozlou is the Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder of Dapper Labs, a blockchain company known for pioneering NFT projects and digital collectibles. Launching his entrepreneurial journey at age 14, he has founded and led over a dozen startups across technology and digital innovation. Known for his expertise in blockchain, decentralized finance, and Web3 platforms, he brings a founder-led perspective on the future of digital assets and financial technology.

speakers.name: Hosam Arab
speakers.about: Hosam Arab is the Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder of Tabby, a shopping and financial services platform designed to provide consumers with greater financial freedom. With a focus on digital payments, buy-now-pay-later solutions, and financial inclusion, Arab has been instrumental in scaling Tabby across the Middle East, delivering innovative products that empower users and merchants alike. Known for his entrepreneurial vision and leadership in fintech, he brings insights on building customer-centric financial platforms, driving digital adoption, and shaping the future of payments and consumer finance in emerging markets.

speakers.name: Faraz Khalid
speakers.about: Faraz Khalid is the Group Chief Executive Officer of noon, the Middle East’s leading consumer commerce platform, which now spans marketplaces for food delivery, quick-commerce, fintech, and fashion. An engineer by training with an MBA from the Wharton School, he brings both technical and strategic expertise to scaling digital ecosystems. Prior to noon, Faraz co-founded and served as Managing Director at Namshi, shaping it into a leading e-commerce platform in the region. Known for his visionary leadership and focus on innovation, he brings insights on building integrated commerce platforms, driving fintech adoption, and transforming consumer experiences in emerging markets.

speakers.name: Akshay Naheta
speakers.about: Akshay Naheta is the Co-Chief Executive Officer of Bakkt, where he drives strategy, product integration, and growth across the company’s unified trading, custody, and payments platform. Previously, he served as CEO of Distributed Technologies Research (DTR), integrating stablecoin and payment solutions into Bakkt’s infrastructure. Earlier, Naheta spent five years at SoftBank Group as Senior Vice President of Investments, working directly with Masayoshi Son on strategic technology investments worldwide. With degrees in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign and MIT, he is recognized for combining technical expertise with capital markets insight to bridge traditional finance and digital assets.

speakers.name: Fadi Ghandour
speakers.about: Fadi Ghandour is the Executive Chairman of Wamda Group, a platform that invests in and builds entrepreneurship ecosystems across the Middle East and North Africa. He co-founded Aramex, serving as CEO for 30 years, growing it into a global logistics leader and the first company from the Arab world to list on Nasdaq. Passionate about impact and social entrepreneurship, Fadi also founded and chairs Ruwwad for Development, a community platform supporting marginalized communities through education, civic engagement, and financial inclusion. Known for his visionary leadership, he brings insights on scaling ventures, fostering innovation, and building sustainable ecosystems in emerging markets.

speakers.name: Abdullah Zahran Al Hinai
speakers.about: Abdullah Zahran Al Hinai is the Chief Executive Officer of National Bank of Oman, bringing over 20 years of experience in banking and financial services. He has held several senior leadership roles and was previously Chief Wholesale Banking and Strategic Growth Officer, where he established the Strategic Growth function and led landmark transactions across investment and commercial banking. Known for his expertise in financial strategy, market development, and corporate banking, he brings a strategic perspective on driving growth, innovation, and institutional excellence in the banking sector.

speakers.name: Claude Marx
speakers.about: Claude Marx is the Director General of the Commission de Surveillance du Secteur Financier (CSSF), Luxembourg’s financial regulator, where he oversees the prudential supervision of banks, investment firms, and financial entities. With extensive experience across regulation and private banking, he contributes to shaping Europe’s sustainable finance and regulatory frameworks and serves on the Board of Supervisors of the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA). Previously, he served as CEO of Lombard International Assurance and held senior leadership roles at HSBC Private Bank Luxembourg. Known for his expertise in regulatory strategy and financial supervision, Marx brings a policy-driven perspective on financial regulation and sustainable finance.
speakers.organisation: Commission de Surveillance du Secteur Financier (CSSF)

speakers.name: Graham Roy Lee
speakers.about: Graham is the Group Chief Executive Officer and Executive Director of Capitec Bank, appointed in July 2025. He joined Capitec in 2003 and has held leadership roles across finance, IT, credit, digital, and data. With over 25 years of experience in financial services and technology across Zimbabwe, the UK, Australia, Nigeria, and South Africa, he began his career in investment banking at Morgan Stanley International before moving into financial software, data analytics, and microfinance leadership. He has also served as a part-time lecturer for the University of Stellenbosch Business School’s MBA programme.

speakers.name: Prof. Michael Mainelli
speakers.about: Michael is a scientist and economist dedicated to advancing society through better finance and technology. Educated at Harvard and Trinity College Dublin, he earned his PhD from the London School of Economics, where he later served as Visiting Professor. In 1994, he founded Z/Yen, the City of London’s leading think tank, renowned for its Global Financial Centres Index and pioneering research in finance and technology. A former Lord Mayor of London (2023–2024) and Alderman for Broad Street, he has advised governments worldwide on financial and commercial centre development, combining academic distinction with public service leadership.
speakers.organisation: President of London Chamber of Commerce & Industry

speakers.name: Andrew Kahn
speakers.about: Andrew Kahn is the Co-Founder of Partners for Growth (PFG), where he has spent nearly four decades structuring custom financing for technology companies. Prior to PFG, he and co-founder Don Campbell led Hambrecht & Quist Guaranty Finance, the lending practice of the San Francisco-based tech investment bank Hambrecht & Quist. Kahn also served as General Partner at JP Morgan Partners, managing venture capital and growth equity funds totaling over $400 million. Earlier, he worked at the Massachusetts Industrial Finance Agency, overseeing bond financing and direct lending for small businesses. Known for his expertise in technology finance, he brings insights on venture lending, growth equity, and scaling innovation.

speakers.name: Melissa Guzy
speakers.about: Melissa C. Guzy is the Co-Founder and Managing Partner of Arbor Ventures, a global venture capital firm focused on fintech innovation. She serves on the boards of Hong Kong and Singapore Venture Capital Associations and has advised the Hong Kong SFC on fintech innovation. Melissa also sits on the Board of Trustees of Jackson Labs and holds board positions in companies including Paidy, TrueAccord, Fundbox, Tabby, EverC, and Planck Re. Recognized as a Top 100 Fintech Influencer and one of Asia’s Most Influential Women in Tech, she brings expertise in fintech, venture capital, and scaling global financial technology ventures.

speakers.name: Rishi Kapoor
speakers.about:
Rishi Kapoor is Investcorp’s Vice Chairman and Chief Investment Officer (CIO). He has been with the firm for over 30 years, having served as Investcorp’s Co-Chief Executive Officer from 2015 to 2024, and as Chief Financial Officer from 2003 to 2015. Rishi joined Investcorp in 1992 from Citigroup.

He holds a Bachelor’s degree in Electrical and Computer Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), and an MBA from Duke University’s Fuqua School of Business. Rishi is a member of Duke University’s Middle East Regional Advisory Board, the Oxford Energy Policy Club, and His Majesty King Charles’ Sustainable Markets Initiative. Additionally, he sits on the Board of Directors for the National Bank of Bahrain, where he chairs the Risk & Compliance Committee.
speakers.designation: Vice Chairman and Chief Investment Officer

speakers.name: William Mason
speakers.about: William Mason is the Director General of the Guernsey Financial Services Commission, with deep expertise in supervisory technology, digital finance, and green finance. He previously led risk and supervision at the UK FSA and the Central Bank of Ireland, playing a key role in reforming supervisory processes post-Euro crisis and contributing to the operational design of the European Central Bank’s banking union. Mason began his career in strategy and energy sectors, and has also served in public office and volunteer roles in the UK. He brings insights on financial regulation, risk-based supervision, and innovation in global financial oversight.

speakers.name: Mark Lenhard
speakers.about: Mark Lenhard is the Chief Executive Officer of Zepz Group, the parent company behind global payments brands WorldRemit and Sendwave, a role he has held since 2022. With over 20 years of experience in fintech and payments, he previously served as COO of Bill.com and CEO of Invoice2go, and led the spin-out of Magento from eBay, culminating in its $1.68 billion sale to Adobe. Lenhard has also held senior roles at JPMorgan Chase and PayPal. Known for his expertise in digital payments, strategy, and growth, he brings insights on scaling global fintech platforms and cross-border payment innovation.

speakers.name: Peter Hughes
speakers.about: Peter Hughes is the Founder of Apex Group, established in 2003 to provide end-to-end asset servicing solutions through local expertise on a global scale. Under his leadership, Apex has transformed the asset servicing industry, delivering integrated solutions to asset managers, allocators, family offices, and financial institutions across the full value chain. The Group leverages innovative technology and a global team of 13,000 professionals across 50 countries. Beyond finance, Hughes supports social impact through the Apex Foundation, championing climate action, women’s empowerment, education, and social mobility. He brings insights on scaling global financial services and driving innovation with purpose.

speakers.name: Sandeep Nailwal
speakers.about: Sandeep Nailwal is the Founder of Polygon Chain, one of the world’s most widely adopted blockchains, and Agglayer, a protocol designed to unify Web3 ecosystems. He serves as CEO of the Polygon Foundation, a nonprofit organization building infrastructure for a truly trustless internet where users retain greater control and ownership of their assets. Under his leadership, Polygon is reshaping how value moves onchain through the development of the Open Money Stack—a vertically integrated suite of services and software enabling seamless, end-to-end blockchain-based payments and financial activity.

speakers.name: Prof. Ronghui Gu
