speakers.about: Professor Ronghui Gu is the Co-Founder and CEO of CertiK and a Professor at Columbia University, serving also as an International Technology Advisor to the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS). He developed CertiKOS, the first formally verified hacker-resistant operating system kernel, laying the foundation for CertiK, now the world’s largest Web3 security service provider with a valuation exceeding $2 billion. Backed by top-tier investors including Sequoia, Coatue, and Goldman Sachs, CertiK safeguards blockchain ecosystems globally. A Yale Ph.D. and Tsinghua graduate, Gu has received multiple prestigious awards for systems verification. He brings insights on blockchain security, Web3 innovation, and trusted digital finance.

speakers.name: Shivashish Chatterjee
speakers.about: Shivashish Chatterjee is the Co-Founder of DMI Group, where he drives the Group’s vision and strategic direction. A 25-year veteran of international financial markets, he brings deep expertise in global fixed-income trading, analytics, and securitized products. Chatterjee spent most of his Wall Street career at Salomon Brothers, later part of Citigroup, becoming one of the youngest Managing Directors and eventually Co-Head of North America’s securitized products trading. Known for his insight in interest rate derivatives, mortgage-backed securities, and market strategy, he brings perspectives on capital markets, structured products, and building innovative trading and analytics platforms.

speakers.name: Stefan Klestil
speakers.about: Stefan Klestil has over 25 years of experience in international payments and banking technology as an advisor, operator, and investor. He is an early backer and mentor to fintech founders including N26, Wefox, Billie, Moove, Curve, Bitpanda, Khazna, Shopup, and Abhi, and has successfully exited Payworks, iyzico, and Holvi. Focused on scaling ventures in Africa, MENA, and other growth markets, Klestil previously served as President of Central and Southern Europe at First Data, Partner at Roland Berger, and Principal at A.T. Kearney. He brings insights on global fintech growth, digital platform scaling, and strategic investment in emerging markets.

speakers.name: Yazeed AlDomaiji
speakers.about: Yazeed AlDomaiji is the Chief Executive Officer of Wamid, Saudi Tadawul Group’s innovation-focused company, driving technological advancement to strengthen Saudi Arabia’s financial ecosystem. With over 17 years of experience in capital markets, corporate finance, fintech, strategy, and project management, he previously served as Strategy, Research & International Relations Deputy at the Capital Market Authority, leading strategic initiatives and market development. AlDomaiji has contributed to national finance committees, including as Vice Chairman of Fintech Saudi Supervisory Committee. Holding an MSc in Finance from Texas A&M and a BSc from King Saud University, he combines technical expertise with strategic leadership in fintech and capital markets.

speakers.name: Dr. George Theocharides
speakers.about: Dr. George Theocharides is the Chairman of the Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC) and Chairman of the Risk Standing Committee (RSC) of ESMA. With extensive experience in financial regulation and oversight, he previously served as CySEC’s Vice Chairman and Board Member. Throughout his career, Dr. Theocharides has contributed to shaping supervisory frameworks, aligning regulation with innovation, and safeguarding investor interests. A frequent speaker at international forums including iFX EXPO, Eurofi, and the Economist Eastern Mediterranean Summit, he brings insights on fintech regulation, market supervision, and the evolving intersection of financial innovation and policy.

speakers.name: Sebastian Widmann
speakers.about: Sebastian is the General Manager of Komainu, an institutional digital asset custody firm, and was part of the founding team that launched the joint venture in 2018. Prior to Komainu, he helped build Nomura’s digital asset team, leading strategic initiatives and investments within the Japanese investment bank. With a strong focus on Web3 innovation, he is actively engaged in exploring and investing in emerging technologies, including blockchain protocols, application stacks, and decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs), advancing institutional adoption of digital assets.

speakers.name: Michael Faye
speakers.about: Michael Faye is an economist and entrepreneur focused on ending extreme poverty and expanding capital flows to emerging markets. He is the Co-Founder and CEO of Taptap Send, a global cross-border payments company, and Co-Founder and Executive Chair of GiveDirectly, where he previously served as CEO for six years. He also co-founded Segovia Technology, a B2B payments platform later acquired by CAB Payments. Recognized as one of Foreign Policy’s 100 Leading Global Thinkers, his research has been published in leading journals including the American Economic Review. He holds a PhD in Economics from Harvard University.

speakers.name: Shrini Viswanath
speakers.about: Shrini Viswanath is the Co-Founder of Upstox, one of India’s leading wealth-tech platforms, serving over 1.3 crore customers across the digital investing and trading ecosystem. With a background in Computer Science from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, he has played a pivotal role in shaping the company’s strategy, product innovation, operations, and technology. Under his leadership, Upstox has emerged as a key force in democratizing access to financial markets in India. Beyond his professional work, Shrini is a strong advocate for fitness and holistic well-being.

speakers.name: Prajit Nanu
speakers.about: Prajit Nanu leads Nium’s corporate and product strategy as the company advances toward seamless, on-demand global money movement. Under his leadership, Nium has achieved significant global recognition, including being named twice to the CB Insights Fintech 250 and featured on the Financial Times list of High-Growth Companies. He plays a central role in shaping Nium’s strategic direction, driving innovation, and expanding its global payments infrastructure to power real-time cross-border transactions for businesses worldwide.

speakers.name: Ian Cramb
speakers.about: Ian Cramb joined UBP in November 2009 as Chief Operating Officer and Member of the Executive Committee. Prior to this, he served as EMEA Consumer Chief Operating Officer at Citigroup, where he began his career in 1992 and held several senior leadership roles, including Risk Manager, Head of Human Relations, Chief of Staff, and COO. With extensive experience across risk, operations, and executive management, he brings deep institutional expertise to UBP’s leadership team. Mr. Cramb holds a degree in Modern Languages from Durham University.

speakers.name: Ashish Mehrotra
speakers.about: Ashish Mehrotra is a seasoned financial services leader who has held senior CXO and Managing Director roles, building and managing large profit centres across diverse product lines and geographies. With extensive experience in complex and highly competitive markets, he has successfully led businesses through credit and economic crises while delivering sustained growth and strong financial performance. Known for driving strategic build-outs of new revenue streams and business transformation initiatives, he combines analytical thinking with disciplined execution. With deep expertise in retail financial services across Asia-Pacific and the Middle East, he is recognized for building high-growth, performance-driven franchises.

speakers.name: Francesco Simoneschi
speakers.about: Francesco Simoneschi is the Co-Founder and Chief Executive Officer of TrueLayer, one of the world’s leading providers of financial APIs powering the Open Banking economy. Under his leadership, TrueLayer has built integration partnerships with major European banks, secured global clients, formed a strategic partnership with Visa, and attracted investment from firms including Tencent and Temasek. An experienced technology entrepreneur, he has founded and scaled multiple ventures across the US and Europe, including Mission and Market, an angel fund backing over 50 startups. He holds degrees from the London School of Economics and La Sapienza University of Rome.

speakers.name: Simon Sharp
speakers.about: Simon Sharp is a Senior Partner at Global Ventures, where he leads the firm’s investment practice and operations. He joined the firm in 2020 as a Principal and brings over a decade of experience across venture capital and corporate finance. Prior to Global Ventures, he was part of the investment team at London-based Committed Capital, focusing on early-stage technology investments. Simon is a Chartered Accountant with the ICAEW, a Member of the Chartered Institute for Securities and Investments, and holds a BSc in Mathematics from the University of Warwick.

speakers.name: Gbenga Ajayi
speakers.about: Gbenga Ajayi is a Partner at QED, focusing on investments across Africa and the Middle East as part of the firm’s international team. He brings extensive experience across fintech and technology, spanning product development, growth, marketing, and strategic partnerships in Africa, Europe, and the United States. Prior to QED, he co-founded Kanza Ventures, a seed investment and advisory firm dedicated to African fintech. He previously held leadership roles at Wise and Revolut, and spent six years at Google driving partnerships, product strategy, and market expansion across Africa and Europe.

speakers.name: Jigar Patel
speakers.designation: Managing Director - Global Head of Fintech & Internet

speakers.name: Omar Shaikh
speakers.about:
Omar is the Managing Director of the Global Ethical Finance Initiative (GEFI) and Board Member of the UK Islamic Finance Council (UKIFC) 
A Chartered Accountant by profession, he specialises in ethical/responsible finance, Islamic finance, and private equity. He has extensive experience in financial services and has advised multiple governments and regulators on ecosystem building as a leading advocate for sustainable and Islamic finance. His background includes working with EY London, where he qualified as a CA and was the former Head of Islamic finance. 
Through his GEFI work, Omar has established a number of initiatives, including the Path to COP, the SDG Financial Products platform with UNDP, which promotes innovative financing structures for the SDGs, the Global Islamic Finance and UN SDGs Taskforce launched with the UK Government and IsDB, and the award-winning interfaith Edinburgh Finance Declaration—the world’s first shared values approach to finance between Islamic finance and the Church. He also served on the Board of the University of Oxford Faith-Aligned Impact Finance Project 
Omar received a BAcc and a PhD from the University of Glasgow along with an Honorary Doctorate from Heriot-Watt University conferred for his work in developing ethical finance. Beyond finance, he is actively involved with Colourful Heritage which works with Glasgow Museums to preserve and celebrate South Asian heritage in Scotland, The King’s Trust Scotland and Olivewood Primary School.
speakers.organisation: Global Ethical Finance Initiative (GEFI) & UK Islamic Finance Council (UKIFC)

track_sessions.session_title: Registration and Networking
Opening Remarks
The Current State of Global Finance: Navigating Economic Shifts in a Multi-Polar World
track_sessions.session_description: Global finance is undergoing a profound transformation as economic power decentralizes and new regional alliances emerge. This session examines how shifting monetary policies, geopolitical dynamics, and evolving capital flows are redefining financial stability and opportunity. Industry leaders will discuss how institutions and fintech innovators are adapting to this multi-polar landscape and what it means for the future of global markets.

track_sessions.session_title: How Global Financial Institutions Are Diversifying for a Fractured World
track_sessions.session_description: As trade, technology, and capital increasingly become instruments of geopolitical leverage, global banks are being forced to rethink where they book business, hold capital, and manage exposure. This fireside chat explores how a CEO leading operations across multiple Asian markets is building genuine diversification — not as a reactive hedge, but as a deliberate resilience strategy for an increasingly fractured world.

track_sessions.session_title: The Liquidity Engine: Building the Rails of Tomorrow’s Value Flows
track_sessions.session_description: This session explores how new liquidity infrastructure — spanning payments, digital assets, market plumbing and regulatory frameworks — is reshaping how value moves across borders and systems. Leaders will examine what it takes to build resilient, interoperable “rails” that can support real-time settlement, scale with global demand, and unlock new forms of economic participation. The discussion looks ahead to who builds these rails, who governs them, and how they will redefine global finance over the next decade.

track_sessions.session_title: De-Risking or De-Globalizing? Building Payment Infrastructure for an Interconnected World
track_sessions.session_description: As global commerce evolves, payment infrastructure is becoming more adaptive, intelligent, and regionally diverse. Financial institutions are rethinking how money moves across borders, building new corridors, strengthening compliance capabilities, and investing in alternative rails. This session will explore how geopolitical shifts are accelerating innovation, reshaping partnerships, and creating new opportunities for more resilient and inclusive global payment networks.

track_sessions.session_title: Towards New Financial Corridors: Capital, Technology and Innovation
track_sessions.session_description: As global capital flows evolve, new financial corridors are emerging, powered by technology and innovative business models. This session explores how digital platforms, cross-border investments, and fintech solutions are reshaping the movement of capital and unlocking new market opportunities. Leaders will discuss strategies to leverage technology and innovation to strengthen connectivity, drive growth, and create more inclusive and efficient financial ecosystems.

track_sessions.session_title: Women Driving the Cutting Edge of Finance
track_sessions.session_description: This fireside chat explores how women leaders are shaping the next frontier of finance, from digital transformation and emerging technologies to new models of leadership and governance. The conversation will unpack the opportunities and barriers facing women at the forefront of innovation, and what it takes to build inclusive, high-performing financial institutions in a rapidly evolving landscape. Attendees will gain insights into how diverse leadership is not just changing who leads finance, but how the industry itself is being redefined.

track_sessions.session_title: Who Controls the Future of Money: AI, Geopolitics, and the Rewiring of Global Finance
track_sessions.session_description: Money is being reshaped by two forces at once - AI increasingly deciding how capital is deployed, and geopolitics increasingly deciding what rails it moves through. This session asks which force actually holds more power over the future of finance, and what that means for institutions caught between the two.

track_sessions.session_title: Lunch Break
Unlocking Growth in a Dynamic Market: Innovations in Investment
track_sessions.session_description: Amid evolving markets and shifting investor expectations, innovation is key to unlocking new growth opportunities. This session explores how technology, data-driven strategies, and alternative investment models are reshaping capital allocation, portfolio management, and risk assessment. Industry leaders will share insights on emerging trends, fintech-driven investment solutions, and strategies to navigate dynamic market conditions while maximizing returns.

track_sessions.session_title: Wealth Without Borders: Serving the Next Generation of Global Investors
track_sessions.session_description: As the next generation of global investors increasingly expects seamless, borderless access to wealth management, traditional models built around single-market relationships are being tested. This session explores how leading institutions are redesigning wealth management to genuinely serve clients who invest, transact, and live across multiple markets at once.

track_sessions.session_title: The New Silk Road: Interoperability as the Engine for Next  Economic Decade
track_sessions.session_description: As trade and capital increasingly route through new corridors bypassing traditional Western infrastructure, interoperability — not size or geography — is becoming the defining advantage for the next decade of global finance. This session explores what it actually takes to build genuinely interoperable financial infrastructure that can plug into multiple regional systems at once, rather than forcing the world to standardize on any single one.

track_sessions.session_title: Consolidation Is Coming: What Fintech's Next Phase Looks Like
track_sessions.session_description: As easy capital dries up, this session looks past the "sustainable growth" buzzwords to the real consolidation wave reshaping the fintech landscape. Panellists will examine which business models are actually built to survive a tighter funding environment, where the next wave of M&A and acquihires is likely to hit, and what founders and investors alike need to rethink about scale, profitability, and exit timing. The conversation will offer a candid, non-consensus read on who consolidates, who gets acquired, and who simply disappears.

track_sessions.session_title: The Intelligent Bank: Redesigning Finance for an AI-First World
track_sessions.session_description: As AI moves from isolated tools to the core architecture banks run on, a genuinely 'AI-first' institution looks fundamentally different from one that simply bolted AI onto legacy systems. This panel explores what it actually takes to redesign a bank's products, decisioning, and operations around AI from the ground up, and what separates real transformation from AI theatre.

track_sessions.session_title: Closing Remarks
Registration and Networking
A New Era of Finance: Navigating the Tech-Driven Economy
track_sessions.session_description: Technology is no longer just a tool for finance — it's becoming the architecture finance runs on. This panel brings together leaders navigating that shift firsthand to discuss which technology bets have actually paid off, which hype cycles haven't, and what genuinely separates transformation from surface-level innovation in a tech-driven economy.

track_sessions.session_title: Banking Transformed: Digital Public Infrastructure for Resilient Finance
track_sessions.session_description: Robust digital public infrastructure is key to resilient, efficient, and inclusive financial systems. This session explores how financial institutions are leveraging technologies such as digital identity, real-time payment systems, and blockchain to modernize financial infrastructure. Experts will discuss strategies for enhancing transparency, promoting interoperability, and strengthening economic resilience while enabling innovation across public and private sectors.

track_sessions.session_title: Digital Banking Reinvented: Blueprints for the Next Generation of Financial Institutions
track_sessions.session_description: Traditional banks are at a crossroads: evolve into fully digital institutions or risk losing market share to agile neo-banks. This session explores how leading financial institutions are transforming operations, customer engagement, and product offerings to compete in the digital era. Experts will share strategic blueprints, technology adoption frameworks, and lessons from successful digital-first initiatives set to shape the next generation of finance.

track_sessions.session_title: Tokenized Capital Markets: What VCs Need to Understand Before Private Markets Move On-Chain
track_sessions.session_description: Private markets are moving on-chain faster than most investors have priced in — from tokenized fund structures to on-chain cap tables and secondary liquidity. This session breaks down what's actually happening beneath the hype: which parts of the private markets stack are genuinely being rebuilt, what VCs need to understand about custody, governance, and liquidity before allocating into tokenized structures, and where the real risk sits for funds moving too early — or too late.

track_sessions.session_title: From Open Banking to Open Finance – How Can Banks Seize The Opportunity?
track_sessions.session_description: The brokerage industry is undergoing its biggest shift in a decade. Retail investors expect instant, mobile-first, low-cost access. Institutions want smarter execution, cross-asset access, and better risk tools. And brokers themselves are navigating new asset classes, from digital assets to tokenized realworld assets, while integrating AI across trading, compliance, advisory, and client engagement. This panel brings together leading brokerage and fintech voices to unpack how the next generation of Brokerage is reshaping revenue models, client expectations, and competitive advantage going forward.

track_sessions.session_title: Lunch Break
How Can Businesses Deploy Innovation at Scale and Responsibly? Scaling AI: Now is where the real work begins.
track_sessions.session_description: Scaling a fintech from startup to global leader requires vision, agility, and bold decision-making. Top fintech CEO's shares first-hand insights into driving growth, fostering innovation, and navigating hyper-competitive and rapidly evolving markets. Gain a rare look into strategic priorities, leadership challenges, and the lessons learned from building a successful fintech in today’s digital-first world.

track_sessions.session_title: Data-First, Digital-Led: Building a Modern Banking Powerhouse
track_sessions.session_description: Behind every 'digital-first' banking claim is a harder question: what does it actually take to run a bank on data rather than just presenting a good app? This fireside chat explores how one of the region's leading banking groups has built a genuinely data-first operating model — from underwriting to customer engagement — and what separates real digital transformation from a modernized interface on legacy infrastructure.

track_sessions.session_title: From Rails to Ecosystems: Reimagining Payments in a Platform-Driven Economy
track_sessions.session_description: As payments move beyond traditional rails, they are increasingly embedded within digital platforms, marketplaces, and super-apps. This panel explores how payment providers, banks, and fintechs are evolving from standalone infrastructure players into ecosystem orchestrators. The discussion will examine the rise of embedded finance, real-time payments, cross-border interoperability, and the strategic partnerships shaping the next generation of payment models in a platform-driven global economy.

track_sessions.session_title: AI-Driven Payments: The Future of Secure, Scalable, and Seamless Transactions
track_sessions.session_description: AI is reshaping the payments landscape, enabling secure, scalable, and seamless transactions. In this fireside chat, a leading expert will explore how AI-driven solutions are enhancing fraud detection, optimizing transaction flows, and creating smarter, more efficient payment systems.

track_sessions.session_title: A Rising New Paradigm: Private Markets vs. Public Markets
track_sessions.session_description: Private and public markets are evolving at different speeds, offering distinct opportunities and challenges for investors. This session examines the rising prominence of private markets relative to public markets, exploring factors driving investor interest, risk-return dynamics, and capital allocation strategies.

track_sessions.session_title: Global Financial Stability in an Era of Technological Transformation
track_sessions.session_description: As AI, tokenization, and instant settlement reshape how the financial system actually functions, regulators and institutions are being forced to ask whether traditional stability frameworks still hold. This closing session examines where genuine systemic risk is building in an increasingly technology-driven financial system, and what safeguarding stability actually requires going forward.

track_sessions.session_title: Closing Remarks
Welcome Address
Rewiring the DNA of Banking for an Innovation‑First Era
track_sessions.session_description: As financial services enter a phase defined by speed, scale, and constant reinvention, banks can no longer innovate at the edges. This session explores how core banking models, culture, and technology stacks must be fundamentally re-engineered to support continuous innovation, ecosystem partnerships, and customer-centric growth in the lead-up to 2030.

track_sessions.session_title: AI and the Next Generation of SMEs
track_sessions.session_description: AI is rapidly becoming the growth engine for small and medium enterprises, reshaping how they access finance, manage operations, and compete globally. This session examines how intelligent tools, data-driven decisioning, and embedded financial services are enabling the next generation of SMEs to scale faster, operate smarter, and participate more fully in the digital economy by 2026 and beyond.

track_sessions.session_title: Why Partnerships Win: How Banks, Fintechs & Big Tech Build Faster Together
track_sessions.session_description: No single institution can innovate at the speed today’s financial ecosystem demands. This session explores how strategic partnerships between banks, fintechs, and big tech players are accelerating product development, expanding reach, and unlocking new value—showcasing collaboration as the core operating model for financial innovation in 2026.

track_sessions.session_title: Reinventing Lending Through Capital Markets Innovation
track_sessions.session_description: Explore how capital markets are reinventing lending for fintechs, shifting from equity-heavy funding to innovative debt instruments that minimize dilution and fuel growth. By leveraging private credit markets, now rivaling public debt at $81 trillion, NAV financing for private equity portfolios, and blended finance with government guarantees, fintechs access scalable capital without ceding control.

track_sessions.session_title: Turning Payment Innovation into Competitive Advantage
track_sessions.session_description: As digital commerce accelerates, payments have shifted from a back-end function to a strategic driver of customer experience, revenue, and differentiation. This session explores how organizations can harness innovations such as real-time payments, embedded finance, AI-driven fraud prevention, and seamless cross-border solutions to reduce friction, unlock new revenue streams, and strengthen customer loyalty. Attendees will gain practical insights into aligning payment strategies with business goals, navigating evolving regulatory landscapes, and capitalizing on the trends that matter most right now—from digital wallets to tokenization.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Break
Smarter Risk, Smarter Insurance: Turning Insurtech Innovation into Profitable Protection
track_sessions.session_description: Insurtech is enabling insurers to move beyond protection toward smarter risk selection, pricing, and prevention. This session highlights how digital distribution, advanced analytics, and ecosystem partnerships are helping insurers improve profitability, launch new products faster, and meet evolving customer expectations in a data-driven insurance economy.

track_sessions.session_title: Building Seamless Payment Infrastructure Across Borders
track_sessions.session_description: As commerce becomes increasingly global, fragmented payment systems remain a major bottleneck to growth. This session examines how modern payment infrastructure—driven by interoperability, real-time settlement, and regulatory coordination—is enabling faster, more efficient cross-border money movement and redefining how value flows across markets by 2026.

track_sessions.session_title: Open banking: a catalyst for financial inclusion and market competition
track_sessions.session_description: Open banking is reshaping the financial services landscape by unlocking data, fostering innovation, and enabling greater collaboration across the ecosystem. This session explores how open banking frameworks are driving financial inclusion by expanding access to underserved populations while simultaneously intensifying competition and accelerating the development of customer-centric products. Attendees will gain insights into how organizations can leverage open APIs, partnerships, and data-driven models to create new value, navigate regulatory complexities, and compete effectively in a rapidly evolving financial environment.

track_sessions.session_title: Lunch Break
When BNPL Stops Being a Feature and Becomes a Revenue Driver
track_sessions.session_description: Buy Now, Pay Later has evolved from a checkout add-on into a powerful monetisation engine embedded across commerce and financial platforms. This session explores how next-generation BNPL models—driven by data, dynamic risk pricing, and embedded credit infrastructure—are unlocking new revenue streams for merchants, platforms, and financial institutions. Positioned in the Innovation Zone, the discussion focuses on scalable BNPL architectures, real-world deployments, and how BNPL is being redesigned to deliver sustainable growth and profitability by 2026.

track_sessions.session_title: From Protection to Prediction: Insurtech for a Risk-Intelligent World
track_sessions.session_description: Insurance is shifting from reactive protection to proactive risk intelligence. This session explores how data, AI, and connected technologies are enabling insurers to anticipate risk, personalize coverage, and prevent losses—positioning insurtech as a critical pillar of a smarter, more resilient financial system by 2026.

track_sessions.session_title: Banking for Everyone: Unlocking Financial Access at Scale
track_sessions.session_description: As financial inclusion moves from ambition to execution, scale has become the defining challenge. This session explores how digital infrastructure, alternative data, and ecosystem partnerships are enabling financial institutions to expand access responsibly—bringing affordable, trusted financial services to broader populations in the global economy of 2026.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Break
AI vs. Human Judgment: Balancing Fraud Detection and Customer Trust
track_sessions.session_description: AI vs. Human Judgment: Balancing Fraud Detection and Customer Trust explores the frontline battle where machine intelligence processes billions of signals in milliseconds, yet human insight catches the subtle cues that preserve relationships and prevent costly errors.This session debates optimal hybrid models—when AI should auto-block, when humans intervene, and how to minimize false positives that drive churn. Leaders reveal playbooks for scaling fraud teams, regulatory compliance, and trust-building tech that turns defense into a competitive edge.

track_sessions.session_title: How Private Credit Is Expanding Lending Horizons
track_sessions.session_description: As traditional lending channels tighten, private credit is emerging as a vital source of capital for businesses and consumers alike. This session explores how private credit markets are broadening access to financing, offering flexible structures, and reshaping the global lending landscape—positioning alternative capital as a key growth driver by 2026.

track_sessions.session_title: Registration and Networking
The Future of Private Capital: Navigating Change, Risk, and Opportunity
track_sessions.session_description: Explore how private capital is evolving in a rapidly changing market. This keynote examines global debt dynamics, regulatory pressures, and investor priorities while highlighting how fintech innovations—AI, data analytics, and digital lending platforms—are reshaping private credit. Attendees will gain a forward-looking view of opportunities and risks in the evolving landscape.

track_sessions.session_title: Private Credit in Motion: Tech-Enabled Megatrends Shaping the Market
track_sessions.session_description: Discover the fintech-driven megatrends transforming private credit globally. Panelists will explore AI-powered risk assessment, tokenized debt, embedded finance, and cross-border lending innovations. This session highlights actionable insights for investors, platforms, and fintech innovators seeking to capitalize on growth opportunities in emerging and established markets.

track_sessions.session_title: From Siloed Products to a Unified Platform: Building Segment-Aware Journeys on a Single Fintech Stack
track_sessions.session_description: This session explores how modern fintechs are breaking down silos to deliver unified, segment-aware journeys on a single digital stack. The discussion focuses on platform architecture, data orchestration, and AI-driven personalization that allow financial institutions to serve diverse customer segments seamlessly, increase engagement, and unlock new monetisation opportunities—turning fragmented offerings into a cohesive, scalable ecosystem.

track_sessions.session_title: Payments Revolution Unleashed: Fueling Global Foreign Trade
track_sessions.session_description: This session explores how next-generation payment solutions—leveraging real-time settlement, smart routing, AI-driven FX, and embedded compliance—are transforming global trade. The discussion highlights how fintechs, banks, and platforms are enabling faster, cheaper, and more transparent transactions across markets, unlocking growth for merchants and enterprises while creating new revenue streams and competitive advantages in international commerce.

track_sessions.session_title: Fintech’s Role in Remittances and Regional Commerce: Driving Credit and Inclusion
track_sessions.session_description: In this session, speakers explore how fintech platforms are expanding access to credit and liquidity in cross-border and regional markets. Experts will highlight the role of mobile payments, digital lending, and embedded finance in supporting SMEs, enabling remittances, and driving financial inclusion—showing the tangible impact of private credit flows on local economies.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Break
Localizing FinTech: Designing Products for Every Market
track_sessions.session_description: In an increasingly globalized world, fintech companies face the challenge of scaling innovative products while adapting to the unique needs of diverse regional markets. Explore how financial technology firms can successfully localize their offerings by balancing regulatory compliance, consumer behavior, technology adoption, and financial inclusion. Gain insights into market-specific strategies, through expert advise and uncover how fintechs can design products that resonate with local consumers, drive adoption, and unlock growth opportunities in emerging and established markets alike.

track_sessions.session_title: AI & ML in Action: How Data Drives FinTech Localization
track_sessions.session_description: As fintech companies expand into diverse markets, understanding local customer behavior and adapting products accordingly is key to success. Artificial intelligence, machine learning, and advanced data analytics are transforming the way financial products are designed, personalized, and delivered in different regions. From real-world examples of predictive credit scoring, personalized wallets, and mobile-first solutions, highlighting how data-driven insights enable fintechs to serve underserved populations, optimize adoption, and innovate responsibly.

track_sessions.session_title: Turning Smartphones into Banks: Mobile Solutions for Inclusive Finance
track_sessions.session_description: This session explores how mobile-first fintech solutions are transforming the way underserved populations engage with finance. Focusing on scalable mobile platforms, digital wallets, embedded lending, and AI-driven personalization, the discussion highlights how turning smartphones into banking hubs can drive inclusion, expand markets, and unlock new revenue opportunities for fintechs, banks, and ecosystem partners.

track_sessions.session_title: Lunch Break
Transforming Credit: Neo-Banks Driving Digital Lending
track_sessions.session_description: Digital-first neo-banks are redefining the credit landscape by leveraging data, automation, and seamless user experiences to deliver faster, more inclusive lending solutions. This session explores how these challengers are disrupting traditional credit models through alternative data, AI-driven underwriting, and fully digital customer journeys, expanding access while improving speed and efficiency. Attendees will gain insights into how organizations can adapt to this shift, harness emerging technologies, and compete in a lending environment where agility, personalization, and real-time decisioning are becoming the new standard.

track_sessions.session_title: Scaling Micro-Credit Globally: Platforms, Risk Models, and Product Innovation
track_sessions.session_description: Financial inclusion in rural and underserved regions is being rapidly advanced through mobile wallets, micro-lending platforms, and localized banking solutions. This session examines strategies fintechs are using to reach these populations, from designing mobile-first products to deploying innovative micro-loans that address local needs. Experts will share real-world examples of how technology is bridging gaps in access, enabling secure, convenient financial services, and driving economic empowerment in communities that have historically been excluded from traditional banking. Attendees will gain actionable insights into building scalable, inclusive fintech solutions tailored for local markets.

track_sessions.session_title: Reaching the Next Billion: Innovations in Digital Lending
track_sessions.session_description: As financial institutions look beyond saturated markets, the next wave of growth lies in reaching underserved and emerging segments at scale. This session explores how innovations in digital lending—powered by alternative data, mobile-first platforms, and AI-driven credit models—are expanding access to finance for individuals and small businesses traditionally excluded from the system. Attendees will gain insights into how to design inclusive, scalable lending solutions, overcome infrastructure and regulatory challenges, and unlock new opportunities in high-growth markets while balancing risk, trust, and profitability.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Break
CX-Driven Innovation: Lessons from SuperApps and Embedded Finance
track_sessions.session_description: This session explores how superapps and embedded finance platforms are leveraging seamless CX to drive engagement, adoption, and monetisation. By examining real-world examples, the discussion highlights strategies for designing integrated financial journeys, embedding services into daily life, and turning customer-centric experiences into scalable growth and competitive advantage for fintechs, banks, and ecosystem partners.

track_sessions.session_title: Cross-Border Payments & Embedded Finance in Emerging Markets
track_sessions.session_description: As fintech adoption accelerates across emerging markets, scaling solutions across borders presents both opportunities and challenges. This session explores how companies are navigating cross-border payments, embedded finance, and regional regulatory frameworks in Africa, Latin America, and Southeast Asia. Hear from industry leaders on winning strategies for seamless expansion, navigating diverse regulations, and capturing new markets.

track_sessions.session_title: Registration and Networking
The Global Paradigm Shift: Beyond Traditional Economic Models
track_sessions.session_description: The global economy is at a turning point, moving beyond traditional growth and development models. In this fireside chat, a leading economist or industry strategist will discuss emerging trends, including technology-driven innovation and sustainability imperatives.

track_sessions.session_title: How Can Banks Unlock New Sources of Growth? A Candid Conversation with CEOs.
track_sessions.session_description: Banks face unprecedented opportunities to grow beyond traditional models. In this candid conversation, industry leaders will share insights on unlocking new revenue streams, leveraging technology, and partnering with fintechs to drive innovation.

track_sessions.session_title: Resilient By Design: The UAE's Future Economy
track_sessions.session_description: As global economic power decentralizes, the UAE has spent the last decade building the institutional foundations to compete in whatever comes next. In this fireside chat, senior DIFC leadership reflects on how Dubai's financial ecosystem was deliberately designed for resilience — from regulatory infrastructure to capital markets access — and what it will take to convert that foundation into lasting advantage as the global financial order continues to shift."

track_sessions.session_title: Building Intelligent Exchanges: Shaping the Smart Market of Tomorrow
Defining the Future of Money: The Next Wave of Innovation
track_sessions.session_description: Money is evolving at an unprecedented pace, driven by technology, regulation, and market innovation. This session explores the next wave of financial innovation, from digital currencies and tokenized assets to programmable payments and fintech-led solutions. Panellists will discuss how these developments are redefining monetary systems, enabling more efficient, inclusive, and secure financial ecosystems, and highlight the opportunities and challenges for institutions, regulators, and consumers in shaping the future of money.

track_sessions.session_title: Tokenization at Scale: The Next Trillion-Dollar Asset Class
track_sessions.session_description: Tokenization has moved past pilot programs into genuine institutional deployment, with projections pointing toward a trillion-dollar asset class within reach. This session examines which asset types are actually scaling on-chain today, what infrastructure and regulatory pieces are still missing, and what it will take for tokenized assets to become a durable, mainstream part of the financial system rather than a parallel niche.

track_sessions.session_title: Lunch Break
Global VC Landscape: Where the Exits (and the Money) Are Actually Going
track_sessions.session_description: A grounded read on where global liquidity is really flowing in 2026 — IPOs, M&A, or the exploding secondaries market — and what that means for where LPs and GPs should be positioning next.

track_sessions.session_title: Unlocking the Future of Wealth Management in the Era of Intelligent, Inclusive Innovation
track_sessions.session_description: The wealth management industry is evolving in an era of intelligent and inclusive innovation. This session explores how technology—AI, analytics, and digital platforms—is reshaping investment strategies, client engagement, and operational efficiency.

track_sessions.session_title: Emerging Markets, Established Ambitions: Where Global Capital Is Really Flowing
track_sessions.session_description: Institutional capital is increasingly flowing toward emerging markets in search of growth and diversification. This session examines where that capital is actually going, what's driving allocation decisions, and how emerging market institutions are competing for global attention.

track_sessions.session_title: Crypto as a norm: Making Blockchain Disappear Into Everyday Financial Life
track_sessions.session_description: For crypto to reach true mass adoption, it needs to stop feeling like crypto at all. This fireside chat explores the philosophy of building blockchain-based financial products so seamless that using them feels like ordinary banking — where the technology disappears into the background and users simply experience faster, more open finance.

track_sessions.session_title: The One-Click Economy: What People Really Want from Finance
track_sessions.session_description: Beneath every feature war in fintech is a simpler question: what do people actually want from their financial services? This session cuts through the roadmap noise to explore what genuinely drives adoption and loyalty — speed, simplicity, trust — and why the 'one-click' promise so often falls short in practice.

track_sessions.session_title: Alpha in a Fragmented World: How Active and Alternative Managers Are Re-Engineering Returns
track_sessions.session_description: As markets fragment along geopolitical, regulatory, and liquidity lines, traditional beta exposure is proving insufficient. This session examines how active and alternative managers are adapting portfolio construction, exploiting dislocations, and deploying differentiated strategies to generate durable alpha in a structurally more complex global environment.

track_sessions.session_title: Closing Remarks
Opening Remarks
Registration and Networking
Capital Markets: Shaping the Future of Finance
track_sessions.session_description: As capital markets undergo rapid transformation, driven by digitalization, decentralization, and evolving regulatory frameworks, they are redefining how capital is raised, allocated, and accessed globally.

track_sessions.session_title: Who Owns the Payment? Banks, Fintechs and the Race for the Transaction Layer
track_sessions.session_description: As banks, fintechs, and platforms all compete to own the moment a transaction actually happens, the real prize in payments is shifting from volume to ownership of the transaction layer itself. This panel explores who is actually winning that race, and what it means for pricing power, data, and the customer relationship going forward.

track_sessions.session_title: Embedded Everything: Where Finance Disappears Into Everyday Life
track_sessions.session_description: Finance is no longer something people seek out - it's built into the apps, marketplaces, and devices they already use. From checkout-page lending to in-app insurance to invisible cross-border payments, embedded finance is quietly becoming the dominant distribution model for financial services. This panel brings together bankers, technology platforms, and investors to explore what happens when finance stops being a destination and becomes a feature

track_sessions.session_title: Move Fast and Fix Things: Fintech's Second-Generation Playbook
track_sessions.session_description: Fintech's first generation was built on a simple playbook: move fast, break things, ask forgiveness later. That era is over. This session examines how fintech leaders are rewriting the playbook for a second generation — one that pairs speed with institutional-grade trust, compliance, and operational discipline from day one.This session will explore what's genuinely changed in how fintechs build, scale, and govern themselves, and why the winners this cycle look very different from the disruptors of a decade ago.

track_sessions.session_title: Governing the Intelligent Financial Sector: Regulation for the Age of Agentic AI
track_sessions.session_description: As agentic AI moves from copilots to systems capable of making real financial decisions, regulators face a genuinely new governance challenge. This panel explores what regulation for an agentic AI-powered financial sector actually needs to look like — and whether current frameworks can adapt fast enough to keep pace.

track_sessions.session_title: The New Economics of Cross-Border Money: The Future of Global Transactions
track_sessions.session_description: Global finance is undergoing a rapid transformation as technology, regulation, and evolving customer expectations reshape how money moves across borders. This session explores the convergence of foreign exchange markets with fintech-driven innovations in payments, remittances, and liquidity management.

track_sessions.session_title: Stablecoins as a Core Financial Market-Infrastructure
track_sessions.session_description: Stablecoins have moved well past their origins as a crypto trading tool and are increasingly functioning as core financial market infrastructure — settling trades, moving institutional liquidity, and underpinning cross-border payments. This fireside chat explores what it actually takes for stablecoins to be trusted as genuine infrastructure rather than just a faster payment rail.

track_sessions.session_title: Lunch Break
Financial Data in the AI Era: Who Really Owns It?
track_sessions.session_description: As AI models are increasingly trained and fine-tuned on financial data, a genuinely unresolved question is emerging: who actually owns that data, and who has the right to profit from it? This panel examines the data ownership, consent, and monetization questions that AI is forcing the financial industry to confront.

track_sessions.session_title: Beyond the Hype: Building Stablecoin Rails Without Increasing Institutional Risk
track_sessions.session_description: Stablecoins promise faster, cheaper settlement, but institutions adopting them are also inheriting new categories of risk — custody, reserve transparency, and counterparty exposure chief among them. This fireside chat cuts through the hype to examine what genuinely risk-managed stablecoin infrastructure looks like for institutional adopters.

track_sessions.session_title: Policy Priorities: Building the Next Generation Digital Economy
track_sessions.session_description: As digital finance matures from experimentation to core infrastructure, policymakers face a widening set of priorities — from data governance to cross-border interoperability to competition policy. This fireside chat brings a policy lens to the digital economy conversation, examining what regulators should actually prioritize to enable sustainable, inclusive growth.

track_sessions.session_title: The New Capital Wave: Private Credit in the Global South
track_sessions.session_description: Private credit is reshaping financing in emerging markets, offering flexible alternatives where traditional bank lending is limited. This session explores the growth of private debt in the Global South, highlighting opportunities for institutional investors, corporates, and development-focused funds. Here we will discuss risk frameworks, market dynamics, regulatory considerations, and case studies of successful capital deployment.

track_sessions.session_title: Closing Remarks
Registration and Networking
Welcome Address
Re-Architecting Market Access in a Digital-First Financial System
track_sessions.session_description: Market access has traditionally been gated by intermediaries, geography, and legacy infrastructure — but a digital-first financial system is quietly dismantling those barriers. This session explores how exchanges, brokers, and market infrastructure providers are being re-architected for a world of real-time settlement, API-driven connectivity, and increasingly global participation. Panellists will examine who benefits as access opens up, who gets disintermediated, and what genuinely new infrastructure — rather than just a digital wrapper on old systems — actually requires.

track_sessions.session_title: Wealth-Tech as the Gateway to Inclusive Investing
track_sessions.session_description: WealthTech has spent the last decade making investing cheaper and more accessible — but access alone hasn't closed the participation gap. This session explores how technology is being used to genuinely bring underserved investors into wealth-building, from fractional ownership to AI-driven advisory that scales personalized guidance beyond the traditionally affluent. Panellists will examine what real inclusion looks like beyond lower fees, and where the model still falls short.

track_sessions.session_title: Tokenization and DeFi: Reshaping Capital Access for Businesses
track_sessions.session_description: Beyond speculation, tokenization and DeFi are quietly creating new ways for businesses to access capital — from tokenized invoices to on-chain credit lines. This session explores which of these models are genuinely reaching real businesses today, and what still stands between pilot and scale.

track_sessions.session_title: Web3 Special Situations: Turning Volatile Markets into Investment Potential
track_sessions.session_description: Volatility in digital asset markets isn't just risk — for the right investors, it's opportunity. This session examines how specialist funds are structuring special-situations strategies around distressed tokens, restructurings, and market dislocations that traditional finance can't touch.

track_sessions.session_title: The Business Model Behind the App: Monetising Neobanking at Scale
track_sessions.session_description: A good app was never the business model — it was the entry point. This session gets specific about how neobanks are actually building revenue at scale: which monetisation strategies are working, which are propping up unprofitable growth, and what a durable neobank P&L really looks like.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Break
Borderless Payments Unleashed: AI + Biometrics at Scale
track_sessions.session_description: As payments go instant and global, identity verification has to keep pace without adding friction. This session explores how AI and biometric authentication are being combined to enable secure, borderless payments at scale — and what it takes to deploy this reliably across jurisdictions.

track_sessions.session_title: Fintech Innovation & Next Big Bet: New Tech That Will Shape The Future
track_sessions.session_description: Every cycle produces a handful of technologies that actually change the industry, and a lot of noise that doesn't. This session brings together investors and innovators to cut through the hype and identify where the next genuine step-change in fintech is likely to come from.

track_sessions.session_title: Hedge That Position: Smarter Investing in the Digital Brokerage Era
track_sessions.session_description: Digital brokerages have made trading faster and cheaper, but risk management hasn't always kept pace with access. This session explores how retail and institutional investors alike are using smarter, tech-enabled hedging and risk tools in an era of instant execution.

track_sessions.session_title: Lunch Break
Fraud Is Getting Smarter — So Must Payments
track_sessions.session_description: Fraud tactics are evolving as fast as the payment systems built to stop them, increasingly powered by the same AI tools defenders rely on. This session examines how payment providers are staying ahead of an adversary that learns in real time.

track_sessions.session_title: Innovation Without Borders: Scaling Finance for the Global South
track_sessions.session_description: Financial innovation built for mature markets doesn't always translate to the Global South's infrastructure, regulatory, and customer realities. This session explores what it actually takes to scale fintech and financial services in these markets on their own terms,

track_sessions.session_title: Solving Today’s Financial Infrastructure Bottlenecks
track_sessions.session_description: Behind every fintech announcement is infrastructure that still doesn't quite talk to itself — legacy cores, fragmented APIs, and settlement systems built for a slower era. This session gets practical about where the real bottlenecks sit today, and what it actually takes to clear them.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Break
Embedded Insurance: The New Growth Engine for Fintech and Insurers
track_sessions.session_description: Insurance is increasingly showing up exactly where customers need it — at checkout, at signup, inside the products they're already buying. This session explores how embedded insurance is becoming a genuine growth engine for both insurers and the platforms distributing it.

track_sessions.session_title: Executive Takeaways & Strategic Insights: The Future of Financial Services
Registration and Networking
Investor Readiness for FinTech Startups: Pitching to Banks & Funds
track_sessions.session_description: Startups often have groundbreaking ideas but struggle to secure funding. This keynote equips fintech entrepreneurs with actionable strategies for engaging investors, from banks to venture funds. Learn what investors look for, how to structure compelling pitches, and how to translate innovation into credible business propositions that capture capital and attention.

track_sessions.session_title: Emerging Technologies Driving FinTech Startups
track_sessions.session_description: From AI and machine learning to blockchain and embedded finance, emerging technologies are reshaping the fintech landscape. This session explores how startups can leverage these innovations to create scalable, market-ready solutions. Attendees will gain insights into the tools and trends powering the next generation of fintech disruption.

track_sessions.session_title: From Chatbots to Financial Super-Agents in Core Verticals
track_sessions.session_description: Digital ecosystems offer immense growth opportunities for fintech startups. This panel examines how platforms—from streaming to e-commerce—are integrating financial services, creating network effects, and enabling startups to scale faster. Experts share strategies for embedding fintech into existing ecosystems and driving adoption across diverse consumer bases.

track_sessions.session_title: Trading at the Speed of Thought: Next-Gen Platform Architecture
track_sessions.session_description: This session explores how next-generation trading platforms are leveraging cloud-native architecture, low-latency infrastructure, real-time analytics, and API-first design to deliver seamless execution for retail and institutional investors alike. The discussion highlights how modern platform engineering, intelligent routing, and resilient systems are transforming trading into a frictionless, scalable, and globally accessible experience—showing investors and operators what it takes to build trading platforms for the markets of 2026 and beyond.

track_sessions.session_title: Trackside VC Bets: From Startup Foals to Unicorn Thoroughbreds
track_sessions.session_description: Spotting payments and lending dark horses that deliver 100x returns by race end. Explores how VCs evaluate, nurture, and accelerate fintech startups from early-stage concepts (“foals”) to high-growth, market-defining “unicorns.” The discussion highlights investment strategies, data-driven due diligence, portfolio management, and ecosystem partnerships that help founders scale sustainably, while providing investors with actionable insights on spotting the next wave of transformative fintech ventures.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Break
Why FinTech Innovation Must Convert: From Interest to Revenue
track_sessions.session_description: Innovation alone isn’t enough—success comes when fintech solutions deliver measurable business outcomes. This keynote highlights strategies to convert early interest into revenue, from product-market fit and user adoption to monetization models that drive sustainable growth.

track_sessions.session_title: Funding, Partnerships & What Excites Investors in FinTech
track_sessions.session_description: Gain exclusive insights from leading investors on the trends, sectors, and metrics that attract capital in fintech. Panelists will discuss partnership opportunities, funding strategies, and the key factors they consider when evaluating startups. Attendees will leave with practical guidance on positioning their ventures for investment and growth.

track_sessions.session_title: From Proof-of-Concept to Production: Why Most FinTech Pilots Fail
track_sessions.session_description: Bringing a fintech solution from pilot to full-scale production is fraught with challenges. This fireside chat uncovers common pitfalls, lessons learned, and best practices for scaling fintech pilots successfully. Experts share real-world examples to help startups navigate operational hurdles and achieve market-ready execution.

track_sessions.session_title: Registration & Networking Coffee
Welcome Address
From Purpose to Progress: Advancing Towards  a Sustainable Global Future
track_sessions.session_description: A high-level keynote on turning sustainability commitments into real-world action through leadership, innovation, and cross-sector collaboration to shape a resilient global future

track_sessions.session_title: From Exposure to Endurance: Financing Long-Term Resilience Through Policy, Capital and Collaboration
track_sessions.session_description: This session will examine how policy alignment, strategic capital deployment, and cross-sector collaboration can strengthen long-term economic and climate resilience.

track_sessions.session_title: Commercial Speaker Slot
From Insight to Impact: Leveraging AI, Data and Technology for Sustainable Growth
track_sessions.session_description: How organizations are harnessing AI, advanced analytics, and digital technologies to transform sustainability data into actionable insights, driving smarter decisions, operational efficiency, climate resilience, and long-term sustainable growth.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Coffee Break
From ESG Disclosure to Strategic Advantage: Turning Standards into Investable Outcomes
track_sessions.session_description: This discussion focuses on how credible ESG reporting and global standards are moving beyond compliance to unlock trust, capital access, and bankable opportunities.

track_sessions.session_title: Commercial Speaker Slot
Accelerating the Energy Transition: Scaling Clean Power, Hydrogen and Sustainable Infrastructure
track_sessions.session_description: This session explores how governments, investors and industry leaders are accelerating the deployment of clean energy, hydrogen and sustainable infrastructure to strengthen energy security, drive decarbonization and enable long-term economic growth.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Lunch
Building Resilient Cities: Sustainable Mobility, Smart Infrastructure and Urban Transformation
track_sessions.session_description: This session examines how innovative mobility solutions, smart infrastructure and integrated urban planning are shaping resilient, low-carbon and future-ready cities.

track_sessions.session_title: Commercial Speaker Slot
The Global Sustainability Transition: Aligning Business Models with a Net-Zero Future
track_sessions.session_description: This session will focus on how global businesses must redesign models, capital allocation, and governance to compete, comply, and create value in a net-zero economy.

track_sessions.session_title: Commercial Speaker Slot
Financing the Future: Sustainable Investments, Transition Finance, and Capital Allocation Strategies
track_sessions.session_description: This session explores how sustainable investments and transition finance are reshaping capital allocation across industries. It will examine the role of financial institutions, policymakers, and corporates in mobilising capital toward low-carbon growth, resilient economies, and long-term value creation.

track_sessions.session_title: Registration and Networking
UAE's Islamic Finance Leadership: Vision 2031
track_sessions.session_description: This opening address sets the strategic tone for the forum, positioning the UAE as a global hub for Islamic finance innovation, capital markets leadership, and ethical financial transformation.

track_sessions.session_title: The Macro Playbook: Islamic Finance as a Global Stabilizer
track_sessions.session_description: Amid global economic fragmentation, this high-level dialogue explores how the asset-backed nature of Islamic finance acts as a stabilizer for the international financial system.

track_sessions.session_title: Islamic Finance Meets Global Ethics: Where Sharia, ESG, and Technology Converge
track_sessions.session_description: As global finance increasingly prioritises sustainability, transparency, and ethical outcomes, this panel explores the convergence between Shariah principles, ESG frameworks, and digital innovation.

track_sessions.session_title: Building the AI-First Islamic Bank
track_sessions.session_description: This keynote explores how artificial intelligence, automation, and advanced data analytics are transforming Islamic banking from the core; beyond mobile apps.

track_sessions.session_title: The "Halal Super-App" Ecosystem: Beyond Banking
track_sessions.session_description: In 2026, the bank is no longer a destination; it is an invisible layer. Explore Banking-as-a-Service (BaaS) models integrating lifestyle, commerce, and finance into a single Shariah-compliant digital journey.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Coffee Break
Shariah Compliance vs Market Reality: Where Do We Draw the Line in Volatile Times?
track_sessions.session_description: A candid and forward-looking debate addressing the tension between Shariah principles and market pressures during periods of volatility.

track_sessions.session_title: The "Digital Amanah" - Automating Shariah Compliance
track_sessions.session_description: This keynote introduces the concept of the “Digital Amanah”: embedding Shariah principles directly into operational infrastructure through automation, data, and governance-by-design.

track_sessions.session_title: Beyond the Paper Policy: The Rise of "Embedded & Invisible" Takaful
track_sessions.session_description: In 2026, Takaful is no longer just "sold"; it is "embedded." This session explores integration into digital ecosystems (travel apps, e-commerce, neobanks) for protection at the moment of need.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Lunch
The Global Sukuk Market: What Will Unlock the Next Trillion?
track_sessions.session_description: This panel examines the future growth of the global Sukuk market, focusing on new issuer types, investor diversification, product innovation, and regulatory harmonisation.

track_sessions.session_title: Crypto Sukuk & Tokenised Markets: Innovation or Inflection Point?
track_sessions.session_description: An edge-of-the-seat conversation blending Islamic finance with future capital markets, grounded in regulation and investor protection, exploring tokenized Shariah assets.

track_sessions.session_title: Networking Coffee Break
The New Issuers: Why Private Firms are Pivoting to Sukuk
track_sessions.session_description: This session explores why private corporates and family-owned businesses are increasingly turning to Sukuk for funding. The discussion will focus on diversification, cost of capital, investor access, and strategic positioning.

track_sessions.session_title: Sukuk Supercharged: Green, Transition, and Tech Hybrids
track_sessions.session_description: A deep-dive on the evolution of Sukuk into green, sustainability-linked, and technology-enabled instruments. The session highlights innovation, investor demand, and impact alignment.

track_sessions.session_title: Taking Islamic Finance to the World: What Comes Next
track_sessions.session_description: A closing strategic conversation on the next phase of global Islamic finance growth — new markets, new audiences, and new delivery models.
